## 2.20 The DEV play API + content verifier

**(a) What it is.** Two QA systems. **The DEV-only play API** (`window.__qa`, stripped from production
via the build's DEV flag): read state, drive the game's verbs (the same typed intents the UI dispatches),
loop control (`tick`/`step`/`frames(n,ms)`, `pause`/`resume`), `new`/`load`/`save`, and **force-state
helpers** (jump to a late unlock / rare outcome / terminal screen). **Canon: expose a DEV-only play API
on `window` so the game can be driven and observed headlessly** (used by the `capture-game-states` skill).
**The content verifier** (`Verify_Game_Objects` equivalent) cross-checks all ids/refs across registries at
test time, and **balance/content docs in `docs/` are GENERATED** from the same data the game runs
(`npm run gen:docs`) — single source of truth. Duplicated derived values in prose are tagged
**"illustrative — see generated"**; gen-docs paths align to **`docs/balance/` + `docs/content/`**, and
the world-sim **`content/world.ts`** registry is generated / verified like the others (Q41/Q55).

**(b) Player-facing behaviour / loop.** None (developer-facing). Powers **headless regression tests**:
force EstateService / **CombatRank** / Influence / a story flag, fast-forward, and assert **each reveal
fires at the intended `GameState`**, that **pacing milestones hit on schedule**, that the **belief→cause
table holds the ≤ 1 ambiguity cap**, and the **V2 invariant battery**: **gate-monotonicity & ceiling** (no
rung needs more than its tier can grant), **accrual tie-out** (deeds sum to the gate share within
tolerance), **each combat track sums INDEPENDENTLY** (level / Arms / Combat Rank never cross-feed),
**trade can never breach ⅓ via a combo** (post-clamp proof), a **real-name DENYLIST lint**, a
**`Math.pow` / `exp` / `log` / `trig` lint** (whitelist `sqrt`), and that **no system ever wipes
Influence/holdings.** The old "no-hidden-edge" check is **FLIPPED** from "labour→combat == 0" to **"each
per-skill perk is SMALL-magnitude (not zero, not a global cap), with conditioning asserted ZERO"**
(Q28/FU8/FU20/FU14/Q36/Q27).

**(c) Rough DATA shape.**
- `QaApi { state(), drive(intent), tick/step/frames, pause/resume, new/load/save, force(partialState) }`.
- `VerifyReport { danglingRefs[], unreachableReveals[], capViolations[], perkMagnitudeViolations[],
  gateMonotonicityViolations[], accrualTieOut[], realNameHits[], mathLintHits[], hiddenEdgeViolations[],
  ambiguityCount }`.
- `gen:docs` reads `core/content` registries → writes balance/content tables into `docs/balance/` +
  `docs/content/`.

**(d) Ties to the four pillars.** Indirect: it **regression-tests the pillar accrual rules** (caps,
high-water-mark gating, dents-not-wipes, trade ≤ ⅓, combos post-clamp) so the four-pillar invariants
can't silently break.

**(e) When introduced / fractal reveal.** **T0 (M0 skeleton)**, grows every milestone. Developer
infrastructure, present from early build.

---

## 2.21 Accessibility, audio & presentation register

**(a) What it is.** Cross-cutting presentation. **Art register: TEXT + EMOJI + CSS + a small curated
audio set + inline-SVG load-bearing motifs + self-hosted OFL fonts.** Load-bearing period motifs (the
**pillar / season / rarity marks**) are **inline SVG** (byte-consistent across OSes); **emoji is
cosmetic-only** (Q38) — correcting the old "no asset pipeline" claim (one acknowledged small motif +
audio asset set). **Audio is "good audio":** mixed **synthesized Web Audio + original / CC0 samples** (the
one acknowledged small curated audio set), with a **mute toggle** (Q50). **Fonts are self-hosted OFL** (no
Google dynamic-subsetting — it breaks offline + the itch relative base; bundle the OFL license, honour the
Reserved-Font-Name rule, Q52). **Accessibility:** solid basics — scalable text, colourblind-safe cues,
keyboard + touch, pause; **responsive desktop + mobile, NOT hover-dependent** — plus the **low-cost
correctness items done now** (Q18/Q48): a **persistent quiet a11y entry point from minute one**, an **ARIA
live-region scoped to narration + milestones ("polite")**, a **large-`textScale` reflow case**, and a
**screen-reader acceptance pass.**

**(b) Player-facing behaviour / loop.** A legible, text-first interface that scales and reflows for
mobile (**per-tier primary tabs + an overflow drawer**, Q19); colour cues backed by text/shape (never
colour-only); full keyboard and touch operation; a pause; ambient seasonal audio with a mute toggle. One
carefully-tuned **difficulty** (no modes). Functional / hint text uses **`--ink-soft`** (passes WCAG AA on
all paper surfaces); **`--ink-faint` is decorative-only**; meter fills are darkened for contrast (Q48).

**(c) Rough DATA shape.**
- `RarityStyle { tier → colorClass + label + svgMotifId }` (colour + text + inline-SVG mark,
  colourblind-safe).
- `A11ySettings { textScale, colorblindMode, reduceMotion, paused, liveRegionScope
  ('narration+milestone') }` (persisted).
- `AudioSettings { ambientVolume, sfxVolume, muted }`.
- Tooltips: a base info layer always reachable without hover (tap/focus), Shift = extra detail.

**(d) Ties to the four pillars.** None directly (presentation infrastructure). It renders the pillar/
standing panels legibly (rarity-coded, scalable) so the four-pillar progress is readable on any device.

**(e) When introduced / fractal reveal.** **T0** — the text/emoji/CSS + inline-SVG + self-hosted-font
register and a11y/audio basics exist from the first build; rarity colour-coding and season tags appear as
the relevant systems (loot, clock) reveal.

### 2.21.1 About / Credits surface

A small **About / Credits** surface (reachable from the persistent a11y / menu entry) carries:
**authorship**, the **commit-SHA build stamp**, **font / audio attributions**, and a brief **clean-room
attestation.** It states the **license split** — **permissive code (MIT / Apache-2.0)** + **reserved game
content** (all-rights-reserved or CC-BY-NC, Q51) — and links the **itch content descriptors** (mild
thematic: child-disappearance, drowning, debt, Q53) as part of the deploy checklist (detail in §7).

---

## 2.22 §2 — items flagged for the human (review checklist)

These are the load-bearing or genuinely-open calls in §2 that should be confirmed before §3:

1. **Pillar accrual, dents & the HYBRID gate (2.16).** Confirm the **two-shape accrual** (achievement
   jumps + seasonal judged results, new-high-water-mark only, per-event capped), the **per-pillar
   recoverable dents with the below-high-water seasonal self-heal (never a wipe)**, and that the **HYBRID
   good/great/excellent per-pillar-per-tier gate** (good in ALL revealed pillars · great in 2–3 ·
   excellent in 1–2; T0 = 2-pillar special; **NO overflow-substitution**; **trade ≤ ⅓ the one hard
   structural cap**, combos post-clamp) is the gate model to build against. *(Balance numbers themselves
   are §4.)*
2. **Trade ≤ ⅓ as a hard structural invariant (2.4 / 2.11 / 2.16).** Confirm enforcing it as a
   verifier-checked invariant (not just a tuning target) is desired — including the **post-clamp /
   combo-can't-breach-⅓ proof.**
3. **Auto-producers strictly T3+ (2.5).** Confirm v1's **E0–E3** estate is **fully active grind** with
   **no** idle producers and **no** assignment/management panel surfaced in v1 (the people-management-sim
   guard) — the "leave it running" feel coming from tab-open **auto-resolve / auto-repeat** (FU23).
4. **Estate build/recruit as light flavour (2.17).** Confirm building & recruiting ship as **diegetic
   reveal beats + light roster cards**, explicitly **not** a buildable management minigame — and that the
   **martial-scale hard-cap** (small named retinue, never an army) is the v1 ceiling.
5. **Belief→cause registry + ≤ 1 ambiguity (2.9 / 2.13).** Confirm keeping **belief-beasts in a separate
   `content/beliefBeasts.ts` registry** from grindable mobs (so canon's "no belief-creatures in spawn
   tables" is enforced at the type level) and that the **single residual-ambiguity token stays at the
   jizō at the weir** (the consolidated find-spot).
6. **Combat surface & quest-types for v1 (2.7 / 2.8 / 2.10 / 2.12).** Confirm combat is **incremental** —
   **exactly ONE starter weapon at T0** + a **growing roster (~9–10 across v1; T0 +2 / T1 +3 / T2 +4)** —
   that **per-skill perks** are the bounded labour→combat cross-feed (conditioning the zero-stat
   exception), the **lean core skills** (farming, foraging, woodcutting, fishing, smithing, cooking;
   conditioning), and that there is **NO fixed quest-type budget** (PEST CONTROL / HUNT / CLEAR / DEFEND
   are the T0 *starting* set, not a cap, Q23).
7. **Standing & Office kanji (2.16).** RESOLVED at the **§5 authenticity pass** (2026-06-25) = **官威
   (*kan'i*)**, "authority of office" (the earlier coined 政威 was rejected). Project-wide, "Combat
   Standing" → **"Combat Rank"** (Q9); "Standing" now names this pillar only; macronize *gōshi* / *rōnin*.
8. **The THREE clean combat tracks (2.8.1).** Confirm character (combat) level (kills/XP), the Arms pillar
   (deeds, phase-2), and the Combat Rank rung-meter (per-rung curated activities) stay **lexically and
   mechanically distinct** (the single likeliest regression is re-fusing them) — the old "Combat Level =
   a Combat Deeds pool" is deleted.
9. **The SEQUENTIAL phase-1 / phase-2 progression (2.15.1 / 2.16).** Confirm every tier is climbed as
   **phase 1 (rungs: per-rung-reset rung-meter + story AND-gate)** then **phase 2 (the four-pillar grind
   to the hybrid gate)**, with **pillar deeds gated to phase 2** (no "half the rungs, maxed deeds").
10. **The MULTI-BACKEND redundant save layer (2.19 / 2.19.1).** Confirm building it **full in M0** —
    IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage, atomic write, app-identity magic field,
    monotonic-save-counter newest-wins (+ timestamp tiebreaker as a documented core-lint exemption),
    additive schema, reject-to-recovery — to survive itch's cross-origin-iframe partition / eviction.

Next: §3 — the incremental unlock ladder (UI-as-progression)._

### 3.4.1 The earned-transition spine — *why* each promotion happens (T1)

> **Audit rule: no rung is granted for free** (mirrors §3.2.1). Each promotion has a concrete in-game
> **trigger** (what you did **for the house**, out in the valley), an in-fiction **reason** (why the *house's
> domain* expands), and a **named granter** — and **the GRANTERS are HOUSE-side** (the **Lord Munenori** /
> chief steward **Genemon**), with the valley's figures (headman **Yagōemon**) **acknowledging** the estate's
> growing role, never conferring rank. The throughline: **the estate's domain expands because you, its agent,
> deliver results in the valley** — survival (T0) becomes *the estate anchors its valley* (T1), standing
> **friendly → TRUSTED**.

| Promotion | Earned by (trigger) | Why the house grants it (its domain expands) | Granter |
|---|---|---|---|
| **V0 → V1** | Run the house's first errands into Asagiri to an Estate-Service rung-meter threshold; open shop dealings (Gonta first) and survive valley pests on the near satoyama | The house, off the foreclosure cliff, must now **operate** in the valley, not just survive on its own land. A man who can carry its business and hold its near-ground is worth recognising → the valley starts knowing "the house's man" | Genemon *(Yagōemon **acknowledges** him at the receiving room — does not promote)* |
| **V1 → V2** | A **Combat Rank** rung-meter threshold — make a stretch of valley road / the ford safe in the estate's name; survive a real bandit/animal clear | The estate's reach can't stop at its gate while its own road is unsafe. Proving you can **secure the valley's roads** extends the house's writ past its fence → it names you its **road-warden** | Genemon (in the **Lord's** name) |
| **V2 → V3** | An **Estate Service** rung-meter threshold — bring the valley economy + the estate's cash-crops to a **recorded seasonal result**; the silk *meibutsu* comes online | Solvency must scale beyond the home fields. You've proven you can run not just the estate's land but **the valley's livelihood** → the house makes you its **steward of the valley economy** | Genemon *(weaver **Onatsu** vouches for the silk hand)* |
| **V3 → V4** | A **STORY** trust beat — surface and end **Magobei's** rice-skim (the doctored *masu*) on the estate's behalf; cross a trust threshold | A house that puts out the valley's fires is owed a voice in the valley's affairs. Resolving the skim **reveals the house's first Office bar** → the four-bar panel lights its third pillar *(its Office **deeds** accrue in T1 **phase 2**, §3.0.1 — the bar lights now, fills later)* | **Lord Munenori** *(Yagōemon, the reachable culprit's patron, **acknowledges** the house's standing in disgrace)* |
| **V4 → V5** | A **Combat Rank** rung-meter threshold — stand a real watch for the valley in the house's name; survive the first dangerous-road encounter; weapon-line milestones; the first paid retinue (Gohei & Yatarō) stands up | The estate has become the thing the valley leans on for safety. A house that **shields its valley** needs sworn arms → the Lord swears you its **man-at-arms** and funds the first (light, flavour) retinue. *(The Naoyuki rivalry→respect beat seeds here — §3.4 V5.)* | **Lord Munenori** + Genemon |
| **V5 → V6** | An **Estate Service** rung-meter threshold **+** a **STORY** beat — the valley visibly settles **under the house**; the alliance/standing levers come into reach | With the valley anchored, the Lord — for the first time — believes the house's impact can reach **beyond** the estate. You are the only agent who has run all of it (errands, roads, economy, office, arms) → he makes you his **right-hand-in-waiting** | **Lord Munenori** |
| **V6 → V7** | The **phase-1 capstone** — the **"clean your room"** beat: the Estate Service + Combat Rank rung-meters topped and the **story** met (estate healthy, **valley anchored under the house**, immediate fires out). *(The Arms + Estate + Office **hybrid** pillar profile is the separate **phase-2** T1→T2 tier-up, §3.0.1.)* | The estate's domain is **secured at valley scale** and its standing is now **TRUSTED**. The Lord, convinced wider impact is real, points past the ridge: the house's domain must expand **again — to the Region** → the **T1→T2 quest** opens; Tomita & Akagi surface as distant rivals | **Lord Munenori** *(capstone — "Now, the region.")* |

The capabilities **stack** (operate in the valley → secure its roads → run its economy → hold an office in its
affairs → shield it → run all of it → anchor it) and the granters stay **HOUSE-side and climb** (Genemon → the
Lord), with the valley **acknowledging** rather than conferring — so the tier reads, start to finish, as **the
estate's domain expanding into the valley**, not as climbing village society. The **V7→T2 gate** is the
ESTATE-domain milestone ("clean your room"): estate healthy, valley anchored, fires out → the lord believes
wider impact is possible → **Region opens**. *(The **village reputation web** runs alongside all of this as a
parallel **optional** track — it can be fully completed and shaves ~10–15% off the climb, but it **never**
appears as a trigger above; §1.5.4, §2.15.)*

| 10 | Ōnaginata / nagamaki | Staff / pole-blade | T2 (G-rung unlock) | weapon-skill |

That's **1 start + 2 (T0) + 3 (T1) + 4 (T2) = 10 line-weapons** plus the 0th carrying-pole ≈ the **~9–10
across v1**. Three **archetype LINES**, each opening a tier apart (Spear T0 · Sword T1 · Staff T2).

**Coupled here too:** **"eat before you fight"** — satiety throttles combat (Q31/FU16), so the reveal of the
combat loop at R3 comes with the satiety→`attackPower` coupling; and the **three combat tracks stay distinct**
(§3.0) — these reveals grow the *character level / weapon-skill* path, never silently the rung-meter or the
Arms pillar.

## 3.6 T2 — The estate's domain expands to the region (full; v1) — `G0 → G7` (a FRESH ladder)

> **What this tier IS (read this first).** Like T1, T2 is **not** "the region track" — it is the **HOUSE
> rising**, a **fresh per-tier rank ladder** (canon §C, §I) on which the Kurosawa estate's standing climbs from
> **TRUSTED → HONORARY MEMBER of the house** (you stop being merely the lord's reliable agent and become
> *family in all but blood* — the house's name spoken in the same breath as yours) and its **domain expands
> again** — from anchoring its own valley (T1) to the estate **effectively leading a region** (T2). Every rung
> is still a **rank within the house's theme**; at every rung you act **FOR THE ESTATE**, carrying its business,
> its arms, and its authority across **all three** scales at once: **the estate, the village, and the region**
> (a cluster of valleys, the post-town **Sawatari-juku**, the upstream **Kuzuhara** ruins, the roads &
> *sekisho*). Region figures — the post-town *toiya*, neighbouring headmen, the castle-town's distant officials,
> and the **rival samurai houses Tomita & Akagi** — **acknowledge, contend with, and finally cede to** the
> house's rise; they do not promote you — **the house does** (§3.6.1), and at the very top the castle-town
> authorities **confer regional leadership** and **invite the house in**. *(Carried forward: **T3** is the
> domain expanding **again — to the castle-town**; §3.7.1.)*

**THREE parallel reputation tracks (only one gates the tier):**

- **(SPINE) Estate domain & standing — the MAIN QUEST.** The `G0→G7` ladder below is **PHASE 1** — climb the
  rungs on the **rung-meter + story** AND-gate (Estate Service / **Combat Rank** sub-tracks; **no pillar deeds
  accrue during the climb**, §3.0.1). Its capstone (**G7**) **opens PHASE 2** — the pillar grind whose
  **hybrid profile is the T3 gate**, now across **4 revealed pillars** (Arms / Estate / Office / **Name &
  Honour**, the Name bar reaching full scoring this tier): **good in all 4 · great in 2–3 · excellent in 1–2**
  (no overflow; numbers → §4). Rising it raises **House Influence** and grows the estate's reach to **region**
  scale. Labour and combat keep interleaving at region scale; the four-pillar panel exists from T0-R7, and T2's
  phase-2 grind drives **Office** to the fore (the "win it socially" steepening, §4.8.3). Estate standing climbs
  **trusted → HONORARY MEMBER of the house**.
- **(SIDE-TRACK A) Village reputation web — carried from T1, still OPTIONAL.** The Asagiri per-shop / per-family
  meters, the headman's **personal** regard, the inn/rumours web, and the **Tama-vs-farmhand allegiance**
  (§1.5.4, §2.15) persist as a fully-completable accelerant — it shaves **~10–15%** off time-to-T3 but
  **NEVER gates the spine**.
- **(SIDE-TRACK B) Origin reputation — NEW this tier (`O0→O5`; see §3.6.2).** Tahei's **living** origin
  community at **Sawatari-juku** is **upgraded from "discrete restored-ties milestones" to a proper one-tier
  standalone reputation side-track with its OWN rung ladder** (elaborates D-009 / canon §"Reputation systems
  model"). It **opens at G2** (doubly-earned: dream-memory **AND** travel-standing), is **optional, fully
  completable, an accelerant, narrative-only with no retroactive backstory gift**, and **NEVER gates the
  spine**. The **Tama-payoff** and the reunions land on *this* track.

> **Only the estate spine (the `G0→G7` rungs + their phase-2 pillar milestones) gates T2→T3.** Village and
> Origin are both fully-completable optional side-tracks — ignoring either leaves you only poorer and lonelier,
> never walled (canon §"Reputation systems model").

Estate stage span: **E2 Recovering → E3 Prosperous** (the **full E3 stage is authored in v1**, Q8/§4.7.5 —
folded into the G-rungs as a koku/Arms sink; **E4–E5 stay parked**; estate fabric runs *ahead* of top personal
rank, on pillars + a **low** rank floor — never the capstone; §1.5.1). **Required pillars drift** toward
**Estate + Office** (Arms secures roads). Ladder shape (LOCKED, §1.5.1 / §5 T2.2): the house's **valley-envoy →
road-captain of the cluster → broker of the post-town trade → arbiter between valleys → recognised regional
retainer → captain of the road-security detail → alliance-broker → leading house of the region** (the rivals
dethroned).

### 3.6.1 The earned-transition spine — *why* each promotion happens (T2)

> **Audit rule: no rung is granted for free** (mirrors §3.2.1 / §3.4.1). Each promotion has a concrete in-game
> **trigger** (what you did **for the house**, out across the region), an in-fiction **reason** (why the *house's
> domain* expands to region scale), and a **named granter** — and **the GRANTERS stay HOUSE-side and escalate**
> (chief steward **Genemon** → the **Lord Munenori** / heir **Naoyuki**), with the region's figures (the *toiya*,
> neighbouring headmen, the **rival houses Tomita & Akagi**) **acknowledging, contending with, and finally
> ceding to** the house — never conferring rank — until the **capstone**, where the **castle-town authorities**
> confer regional leadership and invite the house in (the T2→T3 gate). The throughline: **the estate's domain
> expands to region scale because you, its agent, deliver** — *the estate anchors its valley* (T1) becomes *the
> estate leads its region* (T2); standing **TRUSTED → HONORARY MEMBER of the house**.

| Promotion | Earned by (trigger) | Why the house grants it (its domain expands to region scale) | Granter |
|---|---|---|---|
| **G0 → G1** | Run the house's first surplus past the valley (Kanta's off-books route) **and** survive the region's roads where **Tomita's** brokers already operate | The house, having anchored its valley, must now **trade and travel at region scale** — but the roads are unsafe and the rivals already worked. A man who can carry its business and hold the cluster's roads is worth raising → it names him **road-captain** | Genemon *(the *toiya* and Tomita's agent **Yasubei** merely **note** the new Kurosawa man)* |
| **G1 → G2** | A **Combat Rank** rung-meter threshold — secure the cluster's roads in the estate's name; earn the first *sekisho* pass under the house's seal | The estate's writ can't reach the region while its caravans are turned back at the barriers and harried on the pass. Proving you can move the house freely and safely earns it a **factor's** standing at the post-town → it makes you its **broker** *(and the **doubly-earned** dream + travel-standing conjunction opens the Origin track, §3.6.2 — narrative-only, NOT a promotion trigger)* | Genemon (in the **Lord's** name) *(the **toiya** registers the house — third behind **Tomita & Akagi**)* |
| **G2 → G3** | An **Estate Service** rung-meter threshold — court, supply, and arbitrate **Hibara** + **Tōge-mura** on the house's behalf, out-supplying **Tomita** and out-precedence-ing **Akagi** | Region leadership means the neighbouring valleys lean on **your** house, not the rivals'. Winning them by giving more (never force) proves the estate can **govern beyond its own valley** → it makes you its **arbiter between valleys** | **Lord Munenori** *(the two valleys **lean** the house's way; the rivals **contest** but cede)* |
| **G3 → G4** | A **STORY** beat — reach Kuzuhara with conditioning + standing and commit the house to the multi-stage **river-works (*seki*)** the rivals never touched (the E3 "Prosperous" estate-fabric lever, Q8) | A house that **builds** what others wouldn't — atoning for its own root-sin while re-founding a region node — earns recognition as a true regional retainer, not just a trader. The works are the region's proof the **Kurosawa lead by building** → the Lord recognises you as his **regional retainer** | **Lord Munenori** *(Dowager **Toku** and carpenter **Risuke** vouch; the region marks that **neither rival** did this)* |
| **G4 → G5** | A **Combat Rank** rung-meter threshold — break the brigand roost and secure the trade pass for the region, exposing **Hanzaki** as **Tomita's** hired teeth | The region now leans on the house for **safety**, not just supply. A house that **shields the region's roads** — and cuts off a rival's muscle doing it — needs a sworn captain → the Lord names you captain of its **road-security detail** (hard-capped, canon §E) | **Lord Munenori** + Genemon *(heir **Naoyuki** now turns from internal rival into **ally against Tomita** — earned by the V5 rivalry→respect beat, §3.4)* |
| **G5 → G6** | An **Estate Service** rung-meter threshold **+** a **STORY** beat — broker the region's alliances over the rivals' heads: settle **Akagi** by restoring its precedence and isolate **Tomita** commercially | With supply, build, and arms all carrying the house's name, it can now **broker the region's alliances itself** — turning a proud rival into a partner and boxing in the other. You are the only agent who has run all of it → he makes you the house's **alliance-broker** *(the **Otsuru truth** resolves on the **SPINE** at G6 — guaranteed for every player; the family **reunions** + Tahei's name-reclaim deepen it on the optional Origin track §3.6.2 — narrative-only, NOT a trigger)* | **Lord Munenori** *(Akagi's **Gennai** allies; **Tomita's Sōzaemon** concedes ground)* |
| **G6 → G7** | The **phase-1 capstone** — the rivals' contest decided in the house's favour (rung-meter + the **story** beat: **Tomita & Akagi no longer the leaders** — Akagi allied, Tomita out-competed into détente, never killed). *(The "win the region" **Estate + Office** hybrid pillar profile is the separate **phase-2** T2→T3 tier-up, §3.0.1.)* | The estate-domain milestone: the rivals are **surpassed** and the Kurosawa are the region's **leading house**. The estate's standing is now **HONORARY MEMBER of the house**. Convinced the house *leads*, the **castle-town authorities confer regional leadership** and **invite the house in** → the **T2→T3 quest** opens; the domain must expand **again — to the castle-town** | **Lord Munenori** (house-side rank) *+ the **castle-town authorities** (confer regional leadership / invite the house in) — capstone: "the castle-town invites your house."* |

The capabilities **stack** (trade the region → secure its roads → broker its post-town → arbitrate its valleys
→ build its works → shield its roads → broker its alliances → lead it) and the granters stay **HOUSE-side and
climb** (Genemon → the Lord / Naoyuki), with the region (the *toiya*, the valleys, **Tomita & Akagi**)
**acknowledging, contending, and ceding** rather than conferring — until the **castle-town** confers regional
leadership at the top. So the tier reads, start to finish, as **the estate's domain expanding to the region**,
not as climbing region society. The **G6→G7 gate** is the ESTATE-domain milestone (**rivals dethroned; region
led**) → the castle-town invites the house in → **T3 opens**. *(The **village web** and the new **Origin track**
(§3.6.2) run alongside as **optional** accelerants — neither ever appears as a trigger above; canon
§"Reputation systems model".)*

### 3.6.2 SIDE-TRACK B — the Origin reputation ladder (`O0 → O5`, a one-tier standalone track)

> **What this is.** Tahei's **living** origin community at **Sawatari-juku** — **upgraded** from D-009's
> "discrete restored-ties milestones" to a **proper one-tier standalone reputation side-track with its OWN rung
> ladder** (canon §"Reputation systems model"). It mirrors how T1's village web is a parallel side-track, but
> shaped as a **short laddered arc** (the reunion *is* a sequence) rather than a multi-node web. **Prefix `O`**
> (Origin) — distinct from the estate rung scheme `R/V/G/C/E` *and* the estate physical stages `E0–E5`.

**Hard rules (binding, canon §C / §"Reputation systems model").** The Origin track is **OPTIONAL, fully
completable, an accelerant, narrative-only**, and **NEVER gates the spine** (it is not a trigger anywhere in
§3.6.1). Its **"ZERO mechanical gift" is precisely scoped (Q12):** *remembering the backstory* grants **no
retroactive stat / recipe / combat bonus** — but the track is **not** effect-free: like the village web it is a
**side-faction accelerant** (folded into the **~10–15 %** time-to-tier speedup, §1.5.4) and it lands a
**present-day pride/morale buff** (a *relationship* effect — "a man with people behind him works harder" — and
the small skill-XP nudge that goes with it), **never a gift from the past.** It is **deliberately LIGHT** (6
rungs, not 8 — it must never read as a second spine). It **opens at G2** on the **doubly-earned** gate
(**STORY** — the dream returned enough memory — **AND** **PILLAR** — travel-standing to walk the checkpointed
*kaidō*; §1.5.3). **At least one Origin beat is always available without rep-gating** so the thread never stalls.
**The Otsuru/Tama TRUTH is spine-guaranteed at G6 (NOT gated here, Q5);** only **Tahei claiming his true name**
— with the family reunions + the pride/morale buff — lands on **this** track's `O5` capstone (earned +
**MISSABLE**).

| Origin rung | Earned by (trigger — own meter: **Origin Ties**) | Beat | Diegetic event-log line |
|---|---|---|---|
| **O0 — Recognised at Sawatari-juku** | `STORY` + `PILLAR` (the **doubly-earned** G2 gate: dream-memory **AND** travel-standing) | The track **opens**: the old woman in the street names "Tahei"; the post-town's people half-remember a vanished porter-boy. The **Origin / Ties screen** lights (§3.5). | *"An old woman drops her basket. 'Tahei…?' The street remembers a boy who never came home. A page opens onto people who knew your name."* |

The Origin **pride/morale** buff lands (present-day relationship; **ZERO retroactive gift**). *(Ignoring the Origin track still gives the G6 truth-reveal — you only miss the reunions + buff + the name-reclaim.)* | *"She is real, and grown, and not you. 'Tama ran. Tama lived.' And you have a name again: Tahei. You set it down quietly — and pick your work back up."* |

> **Why `O0→O5` and not `O0→O7`:** the Origin track is a **support arc, not a frontier** — a short reunion
> sequence, kept light so it never competes with the estate spine for the player's attention or reads as a
> second main quest (canon §C, lean discipline). It runs **entirely inside T2** (G2 → G6), is fully completable,
> and shaves a felt-but-small slice off the climb (folded into the ~10–15% side-faction speedup, §1.5.4) —
> **never required, never a wall.**

  rung's eligible-activity rate, §4.1.x), the **HYBRID-gate per-pillar-per-tier band overhaul** (FU10/Q7 —

  triggers' kinds**, never the values. Any §3-implied curve obeys the **`Math.pow` ban → integer-pow**

### §4.2.3 The trade ≤⅓ cap (HARD INVARIANT — not a lever)

**Shape (canon §D — fixed, verifier-enforced §6.6).** Within Estate & Wealth's three sub-engines
(LAND / TREASURY / TRADE), the **TRADE strand's contribution is hard-clamped to ≤ ⅓ of the Estate & Wealth
pillar total**, applied at **every** accrual point (both jump and judged):

```
// WORKING FORM (non-circular — this is the implementation):
tradeAllowed = 0.5 * (land + treasury)
trade        = min( rawTrade, tradeAllowed )
estateTotal  = land + treasury + trade

// PROOF the working form yields trade ≤ ⅓ of the Estate total (the HARD INVARIANT):
//   at the cap, trade = 0.5·(land+treasury)
//   ⇒ estateTotal = (land+treasury) + 0.5·(land+treasury) = 1.5·(land+treasury)
//   ⇒ trade / estateTotal = 0.5·(land+treasury) / 1.5·(land+treasury) = 1/3   (exactly, at the cap)
//   below the cap trade is smaller, so trade/estateTotal < 1/3 always. ✔
```

> **F1 fix.** The previous first line `tradeContribution = min(rawTrade, (1/3)*(land+treasury+tradeContribution_clamped))`
> was **self-referential** (`tradeContribution` on both sides) and not implementable. It is **deleted**. The
> working form above (`tradeAllowed = 0.5·(land+treasury)`) is mathematically equivalent to "trade ≤ ⅓ of the
> Estate total" — verified by the proof block — and is the canonical implementation. The **≤⅓ HARD INVARIANT
> is intact** (and unchanged by this rebalance).

**T0 has NO trade strand (Q29).** There is **no market in T0** — `rawTrade = 0` for the whole tier, so the T0
Estate gate is **100 % LAND/TREASURY** (the village shops are the first market, opening at T1). The clamp is a
no-op in T0 and becomes load-bearing from T1.

**Cross-pillar combos are computed AFTER this clamp (Q22/FU20, §4.3.1)** and operate on the *already-clamped*
Estate total — so a combo can **never** push the trade share above ⅓, and combos are **excluded from the §4.1
gate-threshold check** (verifier-proven, §6.6). A pure-trade run therefore maxes **~⅓ of one of four pillars**
(≈ **8 %** of total House Influence) and can **never carry a tier gate**. The signature *meibutsu* (**silk /
sericulture**, LOCKED) lives entirely inside this capped strand. **The ⅓ ratio is canon and NOT tunable**; the
*raw* trade yields feeding into it (§4.7) are levers.

### §4.2.4 Recoverable DENTS (up-only with minor, scripted, per-pillar dips)

**Shape (canon §D, §1.6.2 — fixed).** Up-only **except** rare, scripted, **per-pillar** dents
(scandal→Name, called debt→Estate, lost battle→Arms), **MINOR & quickly recoverable**, **never a wipe**, and
the dent **never touches `highWater`** (so it self-heals as play resumes the appraisal).

*(proposed v1 balance — for human review)*: a dent removes **`DENT_FRACTION = 0.10`** of the *current pillar
`value`* (max one dent active per pillar), and `value` regrows toward `highWater` at the normal accrual rate —
**clawed back within ~1–2 seasons** by design (canon: "a small dip clawed back within a season or two"). Dents
are scripted story beats only (a finite authored list per tier), **never** procedural/random.

**Below-high-water seasonal restore (Q32 — the explicit self-heal branch).** Each season the per-season hook
(§4.2.2) also runs a restore: if a pillar's `value < highWater` (it has been dented), lift `value` toward
`highWater` by **`DENT_RESTORE_PER_SEASON`** *(proposed ~0.5 · the dent gap, so a dent fully self-heals in
~1–2 seasons)*. This restore raises `value` **only** and **NEVER advances `highWater`** (the appraisal mark is
untouched), so a dent can **never** become permanent and **never** inflates the gate — it simply regrows the
visible bar back to where the high-water already was.

**Levers:** `DENT_FRACTION` (default 0.10), `DENT_RESTORE_PER_SEASON` (default ~0.5·gap); the authored dent list
per tier (count & which pillar). The "never a wipe / never below 0 / never touches highWater / recoverable in
≤2 seasons" rules are **canon.**

## §4.3 Pillar conversion weights (labour / combat / office / deeds → pillar gains)

**Shape (canon — fixed).** Each *protagonist domain* funnels into **one primary pillar** via the accrual
shapes above; there is **no cross-pillar leakage except** the explicit roll-ups (Name reflects the other
three; trade folds into Estate under the ⅓ cap) **and the narrow, broadened T2 cross-pillar combo exception
(§4.3.1, Q22/FU20)**. Skills/levels never grant Influence **directly** — they make the *recognized deed* more
achievable (§2.7d). *(The bounded labour→combat **skillCombatBonus** (FU8, §4.5.4) is a **separate additive
combat-stat channel**, NOT a §4.3 conversion weight — §4.3 still routes deeds→pillars with no skill/level →
Influence-direct edge.)* The "weights" below are the **multipliers applied to a deed's base value** based on
*how well it was performed* (quality/scale), then capped per §4.2.1.

| Domain → pillar | Conversion weight (multiplier on deed base) *(proposed)* | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Combat** → **Arms** | `1.0 + 0.05·(characterLevel) + 0.10·(dangerRingClearedAbove)` | scale of the threat clears more Arms; `characterLevel` = the **single combat-fed character level** (track 1, §4.0.1/§4.6.1); **per-event cap (0.04) still binds** |
| **Labour (LAND/TREASURY)** → **Estate** | `1.0 + 0.04·(skillLevel of the labour skill)` | better farmer → bigger recorded yield jump (still capped at 0.04·band) |
| **Trade** → **Estate(trade strand)** | `1.0 + 0.04·(tradeSkill)`, then **⅓-clamped** (§4.2.3) | silk *meibutsu* graded quality raises the raw, both caps still bind; **T1+ only** |
| **Office/admin** → **Standing & Office** | `1.0 + 0.06·(officeRank) + 0.15·(allianceSealed?1:0)` | alliances (incl. marriage/adoption lever, T3+) weigh heaviest |
| **Recognition/deeds/patronage** → **Name** | `1.0 + 0.25·((armsHW+estateHW+officeHW) / TIER_REF_NAME)` *(normalized by the same explicit `TIER_REF_NAME` as §4.2.2 — no bare `/1000` scaler)* | Name is *derivative* — it lags the other three by design |

**The marriage/adoption lever (T3+, parked):** a one-time **alliance** deed with an unusually large base
(roughly **a full per-event cap** into *both* Office and Name simultaneously) — canon's "real late-game lever."
Numbers detailed when T3 is authored.

**Interaction with the locked deeds-dominate split.** These multipliers grow a deed's *base* with how well
it was performed, but **every result is still clamped by the §4.2.1 per-event cap of 0.04**. So even a
high-skill master cannot turn one deed into a spike — the cap keeps growth in the *many-small-acts* regime
the human locked (§I-bal). The conversion weights therefore tune **which deeds feel rewarding to specialise
in**, not the overall deeds-vs-seasonal balance (that's fixed by §4.2.1/§4.2.2 sizing).

**Levers:** the per-domain coefficients (the `0.04`–`0.25` multipliers). The **structural routing** (which
domain → which pillar, no cross-feed *except* the §4.3.1 combo exception, Name-as-derivative) and the
**deeds-dominate split** are **not levers**; the coefficients that realise them are.

### §4.3.1 Cross-pillar combos (the T2 anti-slump exception — broadened, Q22/FU20)

**Shape (Q22/FU20 — a narrow, deliberately BROADENED exception to §4.3's no-leakage rule; tune-later).** T2 is
the longest tier and risks a mid-tier slump; V2 pairs **two** anti-slump devices: the **seasonal-reward
rotation** (§4.7.x seasonal headline variety) **and** a set of **cross-pillar combos** — small bonuses that fire
when two pillars are *both* advanced together (e.g. a secured trade road that is *both* an Arms deed and an
Estate-trade deed; an alliance that is *both* Office and Name). FU20 broadens the old "single capped Name-only
rebate" lean to **multiple pillar pairs at a larger magnitude.**

**Which pairs combo (proposed v1 balance — for human review, tune-later):** **Arms × Estate** (secured roads →
trade throughput), **Office × Estate** (arbitration → recorded yields), **Office × Arms** (allied retinues →
secured danger), and the late **Office × Name** (alliance prestige). Each combo grants a **combo bonus**
*(proposed `COMBO_FRACTION ≈ 0.02` of the lower-of-the-two pillars' EXCELLENT band per qualifying joint event,
i.e. half a per-event cap)*, stackable but small.

**Proof it breaches no invariant (verifier-proven, §6.6):**

1. **Computed POST the trade clamp** (§4.2.3) — a combo operates on the *already-clamped* Estate total, so it
   can **never** push trade above ⅓.
2. **Kept below the trade-≤⅓ envelope and inside the 70/30 + 0.04 per-event regime** — each combo bonus is
   bounded (`≤ ½` a per-event cap) and routed as a **rebate within** the existing accrual streams, never a new
   uncapped channel.
3. **EXCLUDED from the §4.1 gate-threshold check** — a combo's contribution is **not counted** toward "good in
   ALL / great / excellent," so a combo can **never** satisfy a required pillar or let breadth be bought with a
   pair-bonus. It is pure anti-slump *feel*, not gate progress.

**Levers:** the combo **pair set**, `COMBO_FRACTION` (magnitude), and the qualifying-joint-event definitions.
The three structural facts (post-clamp · below ⅓ · excluded from the gate check) are **not levers** — they are
verifier invariants.

## §4.4 The rich ATTRIBUTE system (STR / AGI / INT / SPD / LUCK)

**Shape (canon §G, §2.7 — fixed).** Five interacting attributes, **stored as base values** (recompute
effective on load = base + gear + milestones, §6.4). Attributes start **near-mediocre** and rise only via
**character (combat) levels** / milestones / gear (mediocre-start). **The no-labour→combat-VIA-ATTRIBUTES wall
STAYS:** labour skills **never** grant STR/AGI or any combat-feeding attribute — attributes remain **combat-fed
only**. *(The bounded labour→combat channel that V2 adds, FU8, is a **separate `skillCombatBonus` addend**
(§4.5.4) — **never an attribute**, never the character level; the two are orthogonal.)* Each attribute has
**labour effects and combat effects** so it is never a dump stat.

| Attr | Combat effect *(proposed)* | Labour effect *(proposed)* |
|---|---|---|
| **STR** (力) | `attackPower += 1.2·STR` (melee); `defense += 0.5·STR`; `hpMax += 2·STR` (§4.6.1); raises carry/durability damage | labour yield `+0.8%·STR`; hauling capacity `+2·STR`; lower stamina cost on heavy work |
| **AGI** (敏) | `evasion += 0.6·AGI`; `critChance += 0.2%·AGI`; hit-accuracy `+0.4·AGI` | gathering speed `+0.6%·AGI` (forage/fish); craft success `+0.3%·AGI` |
| **INT** (智) | `+0.5%` damage vs known bestiary entries; better stance/ability effects | craft quality `+0.7%·INT`; recipe unlock thresholds eased; office/admin deed weight `+0.5%·INT` |
| **SPD** (速) | `attackSpeed += 0.5%·SPD` (faster swings — see cadence §4.6); turn-order/first-strike | action tick-cost `−0.3%·SPD` (faster labour cycles, floored) |
| **LUCK** (運) | `critChance += 0.1%·LUCK`; `blockChance += 0.1%·LUCK`; rare-loot weight `+0.5%·LUCK` | better gather/forage rare-drop odds; market price-swing favourability |

> **AGI glyph fix (Q39).** The agility attribute now reads **敏** (deftness/quickness), not the prior **体**
> (body — which read as constitution); the rename rides the V2 name sweep.

**Starting attributes (mediocre-start contract):** all five start at **5** (out of an early soft-cap of ~30
by end-T0, ~80 by end-T2). The MC's only edge is *temperament* (narrative), **never a stat** (canon §A,
§1.4). **Attribute points:** **+1 point every 2 character (combat) levels** (track 1, §4.0.1/§4.6.1 — labour and
deeds never raise the character level, Q1/FU14), plus milestone grants.

> **NO RESPEC IN v1 (LOCKED by human 2026-06-25, canon §I-bal).** Attribute-point allocations are
> **committed** — there is **no reset / re-spend lever in v1**. Every point spent on STR/AGI/INT/SPD/LUCK is
> permanent; the build you grow is the build you keep. (A late-game respec lever may be reconsidered post-v1,
> but it is explicitly **out** of the v1 balance lock and must NOT be implemented as a v1 dial.) This makes
> the dual labour+combat purpose of each attribute (every attr does double duty) load-bearing: because you
> can't undo a choice, no attribute may be a dump stat — which the §4.4 table already guarantees.

**Levers:** every coefficient in the table; starting value (5); points-per-level cadence; the soft-caps per
tier. The **five-attribute identity, the dual labour+combat purpose of each, the no-labour→combat-via-attributes
rule, and the no-respec-in-v1 rule** are **not levers** (the last is LOCKED by human 2026-06-25).

## §4.5 Per-skill XP curves, per-event caps & milestone perks

**Shape (canon §G, §2.7 — fixed).** Per-skill `total_xp` pools (stored), **levels derived** from a curve;
skills are **hidden until a small visibility threshold** (discover-by-doing); **per-event XP caps force
breadth** (no single action spikes a skill); **milestone perks** at thresholds. Lean core skills at T0; more
unlock per tier. **Every skill (labour + combat) now also carries a bounded per-skill PERK track** (§4.5.4,
FU8).

### §4.5.1 The XP→level curve

A standard incremental geometric curve, shared by all skills (one curve, per-skill *speed* differs):

```
xpForLevel(L)      = round( XP_BASE * XP_GROWTH^(L-1) )
totalXpForLevel(L) = sum_{i=1..L} xpForLevel(i)
```

*(proposed v1 balance — for human review)*: `XP_BASE = 50`, `XP_GROWTH = 1.18`. So L1→L2 costs 50 xp, L10
cumulatively ~**1.2K**, L25 ~**17K**, L50 ~**1.1M** xp. **Visibility threshold** = a skill surfaces in the UI
at **cumulative 30 xp** (~one good session) so it reads as a discovery, not a pre-listed menu. **Soft level
cap per tier**: T0 ≈ **L15**, T1 ≈ **L30**, T2 ≈ **L50** (you *can* exceed, but XP cost makes the next tier's
content the efficient path — this is what stops a player grinding T0 to god-tier).

> **Integer-pow note (Q36/§6.1).** `XP_GROWTH^(L-1)` is evaluated with the **integer-power helper**, never
> `Math.pow` (banned in core) — so the curve replays byte-identically across engines and exported saves.

### §4.5.2 Per-event XP caps (breadth-forcing)

```
xpGain = min( actionXp, PER_EVENT_XP_CAP_FRACTION * xpForLevel(currentLevel+1) )
```

*(proposed)* `PER_EVENT_XP_CAP_FRACTION = 0.25` — **one action can advance a skill at most ¼ of the way to its
next level**, so leveling always takes **≥4 actions** and a lucky big harvest/kill can't one-shot a level.
This is the same per-event-cap discipline canon mandates for combat *and* Influence.

### §4.5.3 Milestone perks (the build web)

Milestones at **levels 5 / 10 / 25 / 50** per skill (lever). Perk magnitudes *(proposed)*:

| Milestone | Typical perk | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **L5** | small flat stat or `+5%` skill efficiency | Farming L5: `+5%` koku/harvest |
| **L10** | a title (feeds Name flavour) + a recipe unlock, OR — for **combat** skills — `+1` related attribute, OR — for **labour** skills — a small **`skillCombatBonus`** perk (§4.5.4, not an attribute) | Smithing L10 (labour): unlocks component-craft tier (a recipe unlock) **or** a small `skillCombatBonus`; Swordsmanship L10 (combat): `+1 STR` |
| **L25** | a `×1.10` multiplier or a cross-skill XP bonus (`+5%` to a *sibling* skill) | Foraging L25: `+5%` XP to Cooking |
| **L50** | a `×1.25` capstone multiplier + a marquee unlock | Combat-weapon L50: stance slot / signature ability |

**No milestone ever reads a returning-memory or porter's-knot flag** (no-hidden-edge guard, §1.4/§2.7c —
canon, encoded at the type level). Cross-skill XP bonuses go skill→sibling-skill **only**. A **labour-skill
milestone never grants a combat-feeding ATTRIBUTE (STR/AGI)**; the labour→combat channel it *may* open is the
**bounded `skillCombatBonus` addend** (§4.5.4), a separate combat-stat term, never an attribute and never the
character level.

> **The bounded per-skill-perk model — V2 FLIPS the old type-level no-cross-feed wall (Q6/FU8, supersedes the
> 2026-06-25 "==0" wall; D-022).** The previous draft enforced **"a LABOUR skill can NEVER grant any
> combat-feed"** at the type level. V2 **relaxes** that absolute wall into a **bounded** cross-feed: **every
> skill (labour + combat) has a perk track (~2–8 perks each, unlocked by leveling it), and a labour-skill perk
> MAY grant a small `skillCombatBonus`** — a **separate additive combat-stat term**, **never** an attribute
> (STR/AGI stay combat-fed only, §4.4) and **never** the character level (combat-fed only, Q1). These perks are
> **stackable with NO hard global cap**; they are bounded instead by **(a)** small per-perk magnitudes, **(b)**
> incremental skill-unlock pacing, and **(c)** holistic balance — gear/level/attrs/enemy-scaling all grow
> together so the labour contribution stays a *minority* share of combat power. **Risk accepted** (FU8).
> **Conditioning stays the ZERO-stat enablement gate** (weak→capable; it UNLOCKS combat rungs but grants **zero**
> combat stat or training-rate bonus — asserted ZERO by the verifier, §4.6.1); the `skillCombatBonus` must
> **not** become a back-door around that gate (kept orthogonal). **Big combat power stays combat-only** (Q1).
> The §6.6 "no-hidden-edge" verifier is correspondingly **FLIPPED**: from asserting the labour→combat edge
> `== 0` to asserting each perk's magnitude is **small/bounded** (per-perk smallness, conditioning still `== 0`).

> **NO RESPEC IN v1 (LOCKED by human 2026-06-25, canon §I-bal).** Where a milestone offers a *choice* (e.g. a
> branch perk, a stance slot, a "pick one of two" unlock, or a perk-track selection), that choice is
> **committed** — there is **no reset / re-pick lever in v1**. Skills themselves still level freely by doing (no
> skill is locked out), but a milestone *selection* once taken is permanent. Mirrors the attribute no-respec
> rule (§4.4): the build you grow is the build you keep. A post-v1 respec is explicitly **out** of the v1
> balance lock.

**Levers:** `XP_BASE`, `XP_GROWTH`, `PER_EVENT_XP_CAP_FRACTION`, visibility threshold (30), per-tier soft
caps, milestone levels & perk magnitudes, **the per-skill perk tracks (§4.5.4)**. The curve *shape* (geometric),
the discover-by-doing reveal, per-event capping, the **conditioning zero-stat gate**, and the **attributes-stay-
combat-fed** rule are **canon.**

### §4.5.4 Per-skill perk tracks (the bounded labour→combat channel — FU8)

**Shape (Q6/FU8 — fixed shape, magnitudes tunable).** Each skill carries a **perk track**: a small ordered list
of **~2–8 perks** (flat bonuses / unlocks) granted as the skill levels (at its §4.5.3 milestones, plus optional
minor sub-level perks). **Data model** *(additive — declared at its milestone, FU5; stored only as the chosen-
perk set)*:

```
SkillPerk = {
  id, skillId, unlockLevel,
  kind: 'efficiency' | 'recipe' | 'attribute' /* combat skills only */ | 'skillCombatBonus' | 'siblingXp' | …,
  magnitude            // small; per-perk bounded
}
// effective combat addend, summed across ALL trained skills (labour + combat):
skillCombatBonus = Σ perk.magnitude  for perk.kind == 'skillCombatBonus'
```

**Labour-skill perks may include the `skillCombatBonus` kind** — a small flat addend into `attackPower`
(§4.6.1) — e.g. *(proposed v1 balance — for human review)* **+0.5 … +2 attackPower per such perk**, with maybe
~8–14 such perks across the whole labour-skill set, summing to a **bounded but un-capped** total that stays a
**minority** of combat power (weapon + STR + character-level dwarf it). **No hard global cap** (FU8): the bound
is the small per-perk magnitude × the incremental skill-unlock pacing × holistic enemy-scaling. **Combat-skill
perks** may grant `attribute` (STR/AGI) or weapon-line unlocks as before.

**Orthogonality (binding):** `skillCombatBonus` is **separate** from (1) the **conditioning** zero-stat gate
(which stays `== 0` stat, asserted by the verifier) and (2) the **attributes** (combat-fed only, §4.4) and (3)
the **character level** (combat-fed only, Q1). It is a fourth, **bounded** input — the gift-vs-work line: only
*trained labour skills* grant it, and only in small bounded increments.

**Verifier alignment (§6.6, Q28):** the "no-hidden-edge" check is **FLIPPED** — it no longer asserts the
labour→combat edge is `== 0`; it asserts **each perk magnitude is small/bounded** (per-perk smallness) **AND**
**conditioning's combat contribution is still exactly `== 0`**. (The two assertions are orthogonal — the bounded
perk channel must not be used to smuggle a conditioning bonus.)

**Levers:** the perk **count per skill** (~2–8), each perk's **magnitude** and **unlock cadence**, which labour
skills carry a `skillCombatBonus` perk. The **orthogonality to conditioning/attributes/level** and the
**per-perk-smallness bound** are **not levers** (verifier-enforced).

## §4.6 The COMBAT math (idle auto-resolve)

**Shape (canon §E, §2.8 — fixed).** Idle auto-resolve + active setup: prepare gear/stance/area → a
**deterministic seeded fight** resolves on a fixed-step sub-tick sim (one RNG, §6.7) → intervene with
stance/ability/item/retreat. **Mediocre-start preserved** (start near-zero; humbling near-fatal first fight;
earned via grind). **Failure = soft setback** (HP/time, maybe drop carried loot, take a rest-off injury) —
**never** lose levels/gear/permanent progress. Combat threads the **three tracks** of §4.0.1 (character level /
Arms deeds / Combat Rank meter) — kept distinct here, never reconflated.

### §4.6.1 Combatant model & derived stats

```
attackPower  = (weaponBase + 1.2·STR + gearAtk + skillBonus + skillCombatBonus) · satietyRate · durabilityBand
                 skillBonus       = 0.3·weaponSkillLevel          (the EQUIPPED weapon line's own skill — combat)
                 skillCombatBonus = Σ bounded per-skill combat perks (incl. LABOUR-skill perks — §4.5.4, FU8)
                 satietyRate      ∈ [0.5, 1.0]                    (§4.6.1a — flat above 0.7·satietyMax)
                 durabilityBand   ∈ {1.0, 0.9, 0.75, 0.55}        (§4.6.1b — weapon wear band)
attackSpeed  = weapon.baseSpeed · (1 + 0.005·SPD) · stance.speedMod · satietySpeedRate   (per-weapon baseSpeed — §4.6.7)
evasion      = 0.6·AGI + gearEva + stance.evasionMod            (an evasion *rating*, not a flat %)
accuracy     = 10 + 0.4·AGI + 0.3·weaponSkillLevel              (an accuracy rating)
defense      = (gearDef + 0.5·STR + stance.defMod) · armourBand (armourBand — §4.6.1b)
critChance   = 0.02 + 0.002·AGI + 0.001·LUCK   (cap 0.50)
blockChance  = gearBlock + 0.001·LUCK          (cap 0.40, requires a shield/guard)
hpMax        = 40 + 8·characterLevel + 2·STR + gearHp
satietyMax   = SATIETY_BASE + SATIETY_PER_LEVEL·characterLevel  (Q47/FU21 — grows with the combat-fed level)
```

`characterLevel` is the **single combat-fed character level** (track 1, §4.0.1 — kills/combat-XP only, never
labour/deeds; M0 skeleton sets `character.level = 1`, FU5). `hpMax` and `satietyMax` both scale off it: *(proposed
v1 balance)* **`SATIETY_BASE = 100`, `SATIETY_PER_LEVEL = 6`** (so satietyMax = 106 at level 1, growing as the
MC levels — Q47). **Conditioning contributes ZERO to every term here** (it is the enablement gate, §4.5.3 —
verifier-asserted `== 0`); the only labour→combat input is the **bounded `skillCombatBonus`** (§4.5.4),
orthogonal to conditioning.

### §4.6.1a Satiety → combat throttle ("eat before you fight" — Q31/FU16)

**Shape (Q31/FU16 — fixed; reverses the old "labour-only throttle, floor 0.3").** Satiety now **throttles
combat**, via a `satietyRate` multiplier on `attackPower` (and a **lighter** `satietySpeedRate` on
`attackSpeed`). It is **flat above ~0.7·satietyMax**, then knees down to a **~0.5 floor** at empty:

```
sat        = satiety / satietyMax
satietyRate     = clamp( SAT_COMBAT_FLOOR + (1 - SAT_COMBAT_FLOOR) · min(1, sat / SAT_KNEE),
                         SAT_COMBAT_FLOOR, 1.0 )                       // 1.0 when sat ≥ SAT_KNEE
satietySpeedRate = clamp( SAT_SPEED_FLOOR + (1 - SAT_SPEED_FLOOR) · min(1, sat / SAT_KNEE),
                         SAT_SPEED_FLOOR, 1.0 )                        // lighter
```

*(proposed v1 balance — for human review)*: **`SAT_KNEE = 0.7`** ("adequate satiety"), **`SAT_COMBAT_FLOOR =
0.5`** (attackPower floor), **`SAT_SPEED_FLOOR = 0.75`** (the lighter attackSpeed floor). This is a **separate
combat coefficient** from the labour `STAMINA_RATE_FLOOR` (§4.7.1) — they tune **independently**. **"Adequate
satiety" = sat ≥ ~0.7** is the reference at which the **LOCKED 20–35 % first-fight win-rate** is measured
(§4.6.6/§2.8/§7 M2a). The floor is bounded so that fighting on an empty stomach never pushes the first-fight
win-rate below ~**15 %** (a bad idea, never an unwinnable one — it's an auto-battler). **Lever:** `SAT_KNEE`,
`SAT_COMBAT_FLOOR`, `SAT_SPEED_FLOOR`. The *direction* (combat IS throttled; ~0.7 knee; ~0.5 floor) is **LOCKED
by human 2026-06-26 (Q31/FU16).**

### §4.6.1b Graded durability & armour bands (Q33/FU17)

**Shape (Q33/FU17 — fixed; it is an auto-battler, so NEVER auto-unequip / NEVER weaponless).** A weapon's
current durability sits in one of **4 integer bands**, each a flat multiplier on its `attackPower`; armour bands
apply **identically** to `defense`:

| Durability band | weapon `durabilityBand` (×attackPower) | armour `armourBand` (×defense) |
|---|---|---|
| **75+** (well-kept) | **1.0** | 1.0 |
| **50+** (worn) | **0.9** | 0.9 |
| **1+** (battered) | **0.75** | 0.75 |
| **0** (broken — still usable) | **0.55** | 0.55 |

Wear is **fixed per FIGHT**, not per swing *(proposed `DUR_WEAR_PER_FIGHT = 2` durability/fight — a cheap
integer tick, replay-stable; armour wears on being hit, same flat per-fight charge)*. **Repair / re-craft
restores to max.** A weapon at **0 durability still attacks at ×0.55** — it is **never auto-unequipped** and the
player is **never weaponless** (the crude carrying-pole, §4.6.7, is the always-available 0th fallback besides).
**All thresholds/multipliers live in `balance.ts`.** **Levers:** the 4 band thresholds (75/50/1/0), the 4
multipliers, `DUR_WEAR_PER_FIGHT`. The "fixed wear per fight (not per swing) / never auto-unequip / repair
restores" rules are **canon.**

### §4.6.2 Attack-speed cadence

Combat runs on an internal **sub-tick accumulator** (§2.8c). Each weapon swings on its **own `baseSpeed`** (the
roster, §4.6.7) — the old single `baseSpeed = 1.0` is **now per-weapon**. A `baseSpeed = 1.0` weapon swings once
per **20 combat sub-ticks** at SPD 0; the `(1 + 0.005·SPD)` factor is 1.0 at SPD 0, ≈1.025 at the starting SPD 5;
each point of SPD shaves cadence per the `+0.5%·SPD` above, and `satietySpeedRate` (§4.6.1a) lightly slows
swings on an empty stomach. A typical T0 fight is **~6–12 swings** (a handful of real ticks). **Lever:** each
weapon's `baseSpeed` (§4.6.7) and the `0.005·SPD` coefficient set how twitchy-vs-grindy fights feel (canon:
low-APM, strategic — keep fights short).

### §4.6.3 Hit vs evasion, damage minus defence (with a floor)

```
hitChance = clamp( accuracy / (accuracy + evasion), 0.15, 0.95 )      // logistic; never auto-miss/auto-hit
rawDamage = attackPower · rngVariance(0.85 … 1.15)                    // seeded ±15%
damage    = max( DAMAGE_FLOOR, rawDamage − defense )                  // the canon damage FLOOR
DAMAGE_FLOOR = max( 1, 0.10·attackPower )                             // chip damage always lands → fights end
```

The **damage floor** (`max(1, 10% of attackPower)`) guarantees even a heavily-armoured foe takes chip damage,
so a fight always resolves (no infinite stalemate) — the canon "damage minus defence (with a floor)".

### §4.6.4 Crit / block

```
on hit:  if rngChance(critChance) → damage ·= CRIT_MULT   (CRIT_MULT = 1.5)
         if defender rngChance(blockChance) → damage ·= (1 − BLOCK_REDUCTION)   (BLOCK_REDUCTION = 0.5)
```
Crit and block are **separate seeded rolls** (§2.8c). **Levers:** `CRIT_MULT` (1.5), `BLOCK_REDUCTION` (0.5),
the chance caps (0.50 / 0.40).

### §4.6.5 On-kill rewards (XP + loot — the THREE-track separation, FU14)

A single kill writes to **exactly the tracks it should, and no others** (FU14 — the de-conflation guard):

- **(track 1) Combat XP → CHARACTER level** = `MobDef.level · COMBAT_XP_K`, then per-event capped (§4.5.2).
  `COMBAT_XP_K = 12` *(proposed)*. This is the **only** thing a kill adds to the character level (which in turn
  drives `hpMax` / `satietyMax` / +1 attr-pt-per-2-levels). **`MobDef.level`** is an **explicit per-mob field**
  (§2.9), defaulting to **~ the danger-ring's expected character-level** (FU15) — it is **not** derived from the
  player.
- **Weapon-skill XP → the EQUIPPED weapon LINE** (a combat skill, §4.5/§4.6.7), separately per-event capped —
  this advances that line's L10/L25/L50 milestones, *not* the character level.
- **Loot** = one seeded roll on the mob's `LootTable` (§4.7.3); rare-tier weight scaled by `+0.5%·LUCK`.

A kill does **NOT** bump the **Combat Rank rung-meter** (track 3 — that is fed by per-rung CURATED activities,
§4.1.1) and does **NOT** accrue a **pillar deed** (track 2) — **EXCEPT** a *recognized* clear/defend (a road
declared safe in the books, the grain-store defended), which fires an **Arms achievement jump** (§4.2.1),
per-event capped, **in phase 2 only**. So grinding boars **never** balloons Arms, the rung-meter, or the level
beyond its own XP — the *recognized* deed does, and only in phase 2. (The old "Combat Level = a Combat Deeds
pool" conflation is **deleted**.)

### §4.6.6 The mediocre-start curve & the soft-setback-on-loss rule

- **Start near-zero, earned only by grind.** At R3 (first drills) the MC has weaponSkill 0, attributes 5, a
  crude starter weapon (`weaponBase ≈ 3`, §4.6.7). The **humbling first fight** (a wolf, §2.8/§2.9) is tuned so
  a fresh MC **at adequate satiety (sat ≥ ~0.7)** wins only **~20–35 %** of the time **(LOCKED by human
  2026-06-25, canon §I-bal — "humbling-but-winnable; drives the player to train"; the "at adequate satiety"
  qualifier added by Q31/FU16 so the locked criterion and the satiety throttle agree)** — survivable by
  luck/stubbornness, never skill; it is meant to be *barely* survived, then motivate training. After ~a season
  of drills (weaponSkill ~5, attrs ~10, a smith-forged blade), win-rate against that same wolf is **~85 %+**.
  **Conditioning contributes ZERO to any of these numbers** — it is a one-way *enablement gate* only (canon §E,
  §1.13, verifier `== 0`). A small bounded **`skillCombatBonus`** (§4.5.4) now *can* exist, but these first-fight
  numbers are measured **without heavy perk stacking** (a fresh MC has barely-levelled labour skills, so the
  bonus is ~0 at R3). Note: under the longer-saga FLOOR, "~a season of drills" is now ~30–40 min of real R3 play
  (the per-rung floor) — the climb from ~25 % to ~85 % is felt as *earned over real time*, exactly the locked
  intent. (Win-rate **bands** beyond R3 → §4.6.8.)
- **Soft setback on loss** **(LOCKED by human 2026-06-25 "as proposed", canon §I-bal)**: a lost fight → MC
  drops to **1 HP** (not death), advances **~½ a day** of clock (the recovery), takes a **random light injury**
  (`InjuryState`, heals in ~**1–2 days** of rest, a small `−10%` to one stat meanwhile), and **may drop
  carried loot** (a seeded roll, **never equipped gear, never levels, never Influence** — the only Influence
  movement on a loss is a *scripted* Arms dent if the lost fight was a defence-deed, §4.2.4). **Never** a
  level/gear/permanent loss (canon §E). The **severity SHAPE** (1 HP + ~½-day + light injury + *possible*
  carried-loot drop, never permanent progress) is **LOCKED**; the exact magnitudes below remain levers.
  **Levers (magnitudes only):** the drop-loot chance, injury severity/duration, the ½-day clock cost. The
  **first-fight win-rate target (20–35 % at adequate satiety)** and the **soft-setback shape** are **LOCKED, not
  levers.**

### §4.6.7 Weapon roster & archetype params + the combat-reveal ladder (FU13/FU12/Q15)

**Shape (FU13/FU12/Q15 — fixed; supersedes the old "3 weapon lines" framing, D-022).** Combat is **incremental
and grows a roster**: T0 **starts with exactly 1 weapon** and unlocks **+2 across T0**, **+3 across T1**, **+4
across T2** → **~9–10 weapons** in v1 across **3 archetype LINES** (each line opens a tier apart). The crude
**carrying-pole is the 0th IMPROVISED weapon** (the always-there "never weaponless" fallback, **not** a line).
Each weapon has its **own archetype params — `baseSpeed` / `reach` / `targetCount` — plus a signature ability.**

**Proposed v1 roster** *(proposed v1 balance — for human review; byte-identical to §2.8/§2.10, §3 reveal rows,
§6.5 `items.ts` — illustrative mirror of `content/items.ts`, Q41)*:

| # | Weapon | Line | `baseSpeed` | `reach` | `targetCount` | signature ability | unlocks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Crude carrying-pole *(improvised — not a line)* | — | 1.00 | 2 | 1 | — | always available (fallback) |
| 1 | **Takeyari** (bamboo spear) | Spear 槍 | 1.00 | 3 | 1 | **Brace** (bonus vs charging mobs) | **T0 start (R3)** |
| 2 | Yari (forged spear) | Spear 槍 | 0.95 | 3 | 1 | **Thrust-through** (chance to hit a 2nd lined-up target) | T0 unlock |
| 3 | Kamayari (cross-spear) | Spear 槍 | 0.90 | 3 | 1 | **Hook** (expose → +crit) | T0 unlock |
| 4 | Wakizashi | **Sword 刀 (2nd line)** | 1.20 | 1 | 1 | **Riposte** (counter on block) | **T1 (opens line 2)** |
| 5 | Uchigatana | Sword 刀 | 1.10 | 1 | 1 | **Iai** (first-strike burst) | T1 unlock |
| 6 | Tachi | Sword 刀 | 1.00 | 2 | 1 | **Cleave** (carry-over damage) | T1 unlock |
| 7 | Bō (staff) | **Staff/pole-blade 棒 (3rd line)** | 1.15 | 2 | 2 | **Sweep** (hit 2) | **T2 (opens line 3)** |

That's **10 line-weapons** (1 start + 2 + 3 + 4) + the 0th carrying-pole ≈ **the ~9–10 across v1**.

**The combat-reveal ladder (FU12/FU4/Q17 — design-staggered, ONE reveal per beat, no UI dump):**

| Beat | Reveal |
|---|---|
| **R3** | the **starter weapon** (Takeyari) + the **bare auto-resolve loop** + **retreat** (§4.6.9) |
| **R4** | **durability bands** (§4.6.1b) with the **loot→craft** repair/re-forge loop |
| **R5** | the **stance slot** |
| **first weapon-L10 milestone** | the line's **ability slot + item slots** (signatures deepen at L25 / L50) |
| **T1** | the **2nd combat LINE** (Sword) |
| **T2** | the **3rd combat LINE** (Staff/pole-blade) |

Reveal staggering is a **design property of this authored schedule** (FU4 — there is **NO `revealQueue` field**
in state, supersedes Q17's runtime-queue framing). **Levers:** every per-weapon `baseSpeed`/`reach`/`targetCount`,
the signature-ability magnitudes, the roster size (~9–10), the unlock-beat map. The **incremental shape** (start
1, +2/+3/+4; 3 lines a tier apart; never weaponless) and **one-reveal-per-beat** are canon.

### §4.6.8 Win-rate bands (the 2nd pacing proxy — Q16/FU19)

**Shape (Q16/FU19 — tune-later).** Beyond the R3 anchor, headless playtests target a **win-rate band** at each
key combat rung, all measured **at adequate satiety (sat ≥ ~0.7)**: **fresh-at-the-rung is humbling** (~**30–45 %**,
with R3's locked **20–35 %** as the lowest anchor) and **comfortable after that rung's intended training/gear**
(~**80 %+**), tightening slightly per tier as enemies scale *(proposed v1 balance — for human review)*:

| Rung | fresh-at-rung win-rate | after intended training/gear |
|---|---|---|
| **R3** (first wolf) | **~20–35 %** *(LOCKED anchor)* | ~85 %+ |
| **V2** (valley road-warden) | ~35–45 % | ~80 %+ |
| **V5** (sworn man-at-arms) | ~35–45 % | ~80 %+ |
| **G1** (road-captain, region mobs) | ~30–40 % | ~80 %+ |
| **G5** (captain of the road-detail) | ~30–40 % | ~80 %+ |

These bands are **wired into the M3/M6 fun-proxy harness** (§7, §6.10) alongside the §4.8 pacing floor — a retune
that makes any keyed rung trivially winnable while fresh, or unwinnable after training, fails the proxy.
**Levers:** every band magnitude (tune-later); the keyed-rung set. R3's 20–35 % is the **LOCKED** anchor.

### §4.6.9 Retreat resolution (Q16)

**Shape (Q16 — fixed).** **Retreat = a clean escape:** the MC **keeps HP and loot**, pays a **modest clock
cost** (a short recovery walk), and **NEVER dents Influence**. The one exception: **abandoning a DEFEND /
defence-deed by retreating counts as a *failed defend*** — which is the only path by which a loss/abandon moves
Influence at all, via the *scripted* **Arms dent** (§4.2.4). Ordinary retreats from HUNT/CLEAR/PEST fights are
free of any Influence movement. **Levers:** the retreat clock cost. The "keep HP/loot · never dents Influence ·
abandoned-defend = failed defend" rules are canon.

## §4.7 Producer / cost curves (gathering yields, crafting, T3+ auto-producers)

**Shape (canon §G — fixed).** **Auto-producers are LATE-GAME (T3+) ONLY** (§2.5); v1's T0–T2 is the MC's own
**active grind** — so for v1 the load-bearing curves are **gathering yields**, **crafting costs/quality**, and
**building/upgrade costs**. The genre-standard `cost = base · r^owned` is used **only** for the late-game
auto-producers and for repeatable upgrades, **never** to fake an early idle layer. **v1 is active-only (no
offline accrual, FU23)** — the "leave it running, check progress" feel comes from **tab-open auto-resolve combat
+ auto-repeat labour**, not an offline layer.

### §4.7.1 Gathering / labour yields (active)

```
yieldPerAction = baseYield · (1 + 0.04·skillLevel) · toolMult · seasonMult · weatherMult · staminaRate
ticksPerAction = baseTicks · (1 − 0.003·SPD)   (floored at 1)
```

**Yields are "already net" (Q14)** — `baseYield` is stated net of routine upkeep, so the lifetime-produced koku
translates almost directly into held koku (the §4.0 NET-held figure). *(proposed v1 base yields, koku/material
per action — UNCHANGED in magnitude from the first pass; the longer saga is paid in TIME at the per-rung floor,
not in shrunk yields)*:

| Node | baseYield | baseTicks | toolMult range | season gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Rake/farm rice (paddy) | 2 koku | 3 | 1.0 → 2.5 (sickle→fine tools) | grows spring/summer, **harvest autumn** |
| Forage *sansai* | 1 greens | 2 | 1.0 → 1.8 | spring/summer windows |
| Woodcut | 2 wood | 3 | 1.0 → 2.2 | year-round |
| Fish (ford) | 1 fish | 2 | 1.0 → 2.0 | year-round, peak autumn |
| Sericulture (silk, T1+) | 1 cocoon | 4 | 1.0 → 3.0 | **summer** (mulberry) |

**Labour stamina throttle (FU21).** `staminaRate` is the labour analogue of §4.6.1a: **flat above ~0.7·satietyMax
→ a ~0.5 floor** *(proposed `STAMINA_RATE_FLOOR = 0.5`, `STAMINA_KNEE = 0.7`)*, with a **modest drain per action**
and **rest refilling** satiety. This is a **separate coefficient** from the combat `satietyRate` (§4.6.1a) — they
tune independently. (`satietyMax = base + per-level growth`, §4.6.1.)

**Mechanical weather/festival modifier (Q35/FU3 — bounded ±10 %, derived & STATELESS).** `weatherMult` is a
bounded **±10 %** mechanical modifier (a good/bad weather day, a festival) read from a **stateless day-keyed
helper** `deriveDayKeyed(seed, 'weather', worldClock.dayIndex)` — **never persisted** (FU3; only market-
saturation persists as world-sim state, §6.7.1). Festivals modulate the *judged-result* basis ±10 % too
(§4.2.2). The modifier is bounded so it's flavourful, never a swing that breaks pacing.

**Throughput tie-out (how these yields produce the §4.8 pacing).** A T0 rice action runs
`2·(1+0.04·skill)·toolMult` koku. At **R1** (skill ~1, crude tools 1.0) that's **~2.1 koku/action**; at
**R7** (skill ~12, fine tools 2.0) it's **~5.9 koku/action**. At the intended active pace, the *net
koku-equivalent throughput* rises across T0's rungs roughly: **R1 ~25 → R2 ~35 → R3 ~40 → R4 ~60 → R5 ~80 → R6
~110 → R7 ~150 koku/min** (combat rungs trade some labour minutes for loot value; crafting/cash-crop rungs add
value per action). Multiplying each by its rung's wall-clock minutes (§4.8) gives **lifetime-produced koku over
T0 ≈ 21K**, and because yields are already net (Q14), **NET held ≈ 18–19K** after the modest E1/E2 build + tool/
food spend — which is the §4.0 T0 band, and which clears the E1/E2 building costs (§4.7.5) on schedule. **This
throughput table is the bridge between the yields here and the rung-time table in §4.8; retune the
`0.04·skillLevel` slope and tool multipliers, never the per-rung time floor, to fix pacing drift.**

**Seasonal headline:** the autumn rice harvest is a **`×3` season multiplier** on paddy yield (drives the
Estate judged-result high-water mark, §4.2.2 — the autumn appraisal is the one most likely to set a fresh
mark). **Levers:** every baseYield/baseTicks, the `0.04·skillLevel` and `0.003·SPD` coefficients, tool
multipliers, season multipliers, the autumn `×3`, `weatherMult` bound (±10 %), `STAMINA_RATE_FLOOR`/`STAMINA_KNEE`,
and the **per-rung net-throughput assumptions** (§4.8).

### §4.7.2 Crafting cost & quality (hybrid: simple → component)

- **Simple recipes (early):** flat `inputs → output`; cost a fixed small material bundle; success ~100%.
- **Component recipes (T1+):** `outputQualityTier = floor( (crafterSkill·0.4 + avgComponentQuality·0.4 +
  stationTier·0.2) / QUALITY_DIVISOR )`, `QUALITY_DIVISOR = 10` → quality tiers **0–5** (crude → masterwork).
  Each quality tier multiplies the item's stat/value by **`1.25^tier`** *(evaluated with the integer-power
  helper, never `Math.pow` — Q36/§6.1)*. Disassembly returns **~60%** of materials. **Levers:** the 0.4/0.4/0.2
  quality weights, `QUALITY_DIVISOR`, the `1.25^tier` value step, the 60% disassembly return. *(Crafting
  magnitudes are unchanged by the rebalance — the value-add they provide is already folded into the rising
  per-rung throughput in §4.7.1/§4.8.)*

### §4.7.3 Loot tables (seeded) & market notes

A mob/node drops on a weighted table; **rarity tiers common/uncommon/rare/fine** with default weights
**70 / 22 / 6 / 2** (rare/fine weight scaled by `+0.5%·LUCK`). **Levers:** the rarity weights and the LUCK
coefficient.

**Market / coin notes (Q13/Q42 — M4 placeholders, NOT frozen).** The **koku↔coin spread, the single coin sink,
and `MarketState`** are **M4 placeholders** (Q13) — provisional numbers, to be set when the village market is
built; **resource counts stay unbounded** (no "+caps"). Bulk sales apply the **saturation damper PROGRESSIVELY
per-unit** (Q42): each unit sold walks the price down a notch (rather than a flat post-hoc multiplier), so the
damper is **legible and un-gameable** — selling 100 silk in one click yields exactly what 100 single sales would.
The persisted world-sim state behind this is **market-saturation only** (§6.7.1).

### §4.7.4 Auto-producers (T3+ ONLY — parked, scaffold only; ACTIVE-ONLY)

```
cost(n)  = producerBase · PRODUCER_GROWTH^owned          (PRODUCER_GROWTH = 1.5, ~5× per few buys; integer-pow)
output   = producerBaseRate · (1 + 0.1·tier)              (per active tick — NO offline accrual, canon §H / FU23)
```

Gated on **Influence band + a LOW rank floor + cost** (NOT the capstone — §1.5.1 estate-growth rule), bound to
a `RosterMember` (a face, not a slider; §2.5). **All values parked until T3 is authored**; only the
**shape/`PRODUCER_GROWTH` scaffold** is fixed here. **Active-only is reaffirmed (FU23):** there is **no offline
accrual** anywhere in v1 — the "leave it running, check progress" feel is delivered by **tab-open auto-resolve
combat + auto-repeat labour** (active-only), and auto-producers remain a **T3+** feature.

### §4.7.5 Building / upgrade costs (estate stages E0→E3, v1 — Q8)

Estate physical stages gate on **a LOW rank floor + koku/material cost** (the binding *phase-reachable* gate),
with the §1.5.1 **Estate & Wealth pillar floor** binding **once Influence is accruing (phase 2)** — never the
capstone. **E1/E2 fall in the T0 phase-1 climb** (gated by rank + koku); **E3 "Prosperous" is a T2 phase-2 sink**
(Q8 — un-parks E3; the estate now grows **E0→E3** in v1, with **E4–E5 still parked**). *(proposed)*:

| Stage | Gate (rank floor + cost; pillar floor binds in phase 2) | koku cost | material cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| **E0 Foreclosure's Edge** | start | — | — |
| **E1 Stabilising** (kura patched, first *shinden*, drill yard, night-watch) | rank ≥ R4 (Estate floor ~0.3K once phase 2) | 400 koku | 30 wood |
| **E2 Recovering** (granary, 2 workshops, low palisade, men-at-arms rota) | rank ≥ R6 (Estate ~0.6K + Arms ~0.3K once phase 2) | 2K koku | 120 wood, 40 stone |
| **E3 Prosperous** (full granary complex, kawara roofs, walled compound, standing retinue) | rank ≥ G4 + **Estate ~40K + Arms ~20K** (phase-2 floors) | **~80K koku** *(proposed v1 balance)* | 600 wood, 250 stone, 60 tile |

**E3 folds into the later G-rungs as an extra koku/Arms SINK — it adds NO new rung** (FU18): it is a *build/
authoring* cost that gives T2's koku-mountain something to buy, while **play-time stays a FLOOR** (E3 doesn't
lengthen the rung ladder, it deepens the sink). **Affordability tie-out.** E1's **400 koku** falls due around
**R4**, by which point lifetime-produced koku ≈ **5.1K**; E2's **2K** around **R6** (≈ **13.3K** produced) —
both **paced to be reached, not pre-bought**, with end-T0 **NET held ≈ 18–19K** (Q14) leaving comfortable
headroom. E3's **~80K** is a **T2-scale** sink (T2 produces into the low-M koku band, §4.0), comfortably
affordable within T2 while still being a meaningful drain on the region-scale treasury. **E4–E5 parked** (§1.5.1).
**Levers:** every cost & rank/pillar floor above (all *proposed*), incl. the E3 row.

## §4.8 PACING targets — the time-budget FLOOR + the rung-by-rank pacing tables

These are the **playtest acceptance criteria** the numbers above are derived *backward from*; they are
headlessly regression-tested via the DEV play API (§6.10) so a retune that breaks pacing fails CI. **Per FU18 the
per-tier budgets are FLOORS, not ceilings** — the game can run **longer** (a long OSRS-rough grind); the M6
pacing gate fails on **UNDERSHOOT ONLY**. Canon hard rules: **first action < 5 s**, **the next goal never
balloons > ~2–3× the prior** (within a tier; canon §G, §1.2), and — **LOCKED by human 2026-06-25 (canon §I-bal),
re-scoped as FLOORS by FU18** — **every single rung takes ≥ ~30 min**, and the **per-tier budgets are T0 ≈ 4.5 h
· T1 ≈ 8 h · T2 ≈ 16 h** ("more grind, more numbers, a slower release of incremental features"). **v1 total
(T0–T2) ≈ 28.5 h FLOOR** (the canon "≈32 h" figure includes the T3-stub runway/free-play tail). **Each tier =
phase-1 rungs + phase-2 pillar grind** (§4.0.1); the per-tier figure below is the **phase-1 climb FLOOR**, and the
**phase-2 grind runs on top**, so realized time-to-next-tier ≥ the floor (exactly FU18's intended lengthening).

### §4.8.0 Headline beats

| Beat | Target | Lock status | How measured |
|---|---|---|---|
| **First action available** | **< 5 s** from load (rake spilled rice in the kura) | canon | time-to-first-interactable |
| **First meaningful reveal** | **< 30 s** (rice counter ticks → the koku row lights its own panel, §3.1) | proposed | first `unlock` event |
| **Per-rung minimum** | **≥ ~30 min per rung** (no rung advances faster) | **LOCKED 2026-06-25 (FLOOR)** | per-rung tick-count floor |
| **Humbling first fight (R3)** | **~60–75 min** in (start of R3) | proposed | tick-count to the wolf encounter |
| **First-fight win-rate** | **20–35 % at adequate satiety (sat ≥ ~0.7)** | **LOCKED (anchor)** | headless win-rate at R3 |
| **Win-rate bands** | fresh-at-rung ~30–45 % / trained ~80 %+ at R3/V2/V5/G1/G5 (§4.6.8) | proposed (tune-later) | headless win-rate per keyed rung |
| **Time-to-T1** (sent to village) | **≥ ≈ 4.5 h** active play (FLOOR) | **LOCKED (T0 budget = floor)** | tick-count to T0→T1 gate |
| **Time-to-T2** (region opens) | **≥ ≈ 12.5 h** cumulative (4.5 + 8) (FLOOR) | **LOCKED (T1 budget = floor)** | tick-count to T1→T2 gate |
| **v1 completion** (T2→T3 gate / stub) | **≥ ≈ 28.5 h** cumulative (4.5 + 8 + 16) (FLOOR) | **LOCKED (T2 budget = floor)** | tick-count to T2→T3 gate |
| **Goal-to-goal step ratio** | **≤ 2–3×** between consecutive within-tier goals | canon | max ratio of consecutive costs |
| **Tier-to-tier step** | **~10×** (Arms/Estate) — a chapter, not a wall; Office steepens | proposed | band-top ratio across tiers |
| **Side-faction speedup** | village+origin weaving **shaves ~10–15 %** time-to-next-tier (felt, never required) | proposed | with/without multipliers |

> The old draft's "leave T0 in ~45–75 min; v1 ~12–20 h" row is **deleted** — superseded by the locked
> longer budget above (now a FLOOR).

### §4.8.1 ⭐ T0 pacing — Phase-1 rung climb (the centrepiece; all 8 rungs, full resolution)

This is the table the rest of §4 is tuned to satisfy. **Each rung's expected wall-clock ≥ ~30 min (R0 aside, the

crossings, §4.1.1). *(Times & costs: proposed FLOORS; the ≥30-min floor and the ≈4.5-h phase-1 sum: **LOCKED as
FLOORS**.)*
