# T4 — Edo (ROADMAP)

> **v1 ships a ROADMAP only** (canon §I) — the national tier is scoped forward, not built. All beats below are
> the authored target, kept lean so a later milestone can build against them.

## T4.1 Overview (roadmap)

**Theme:** *recognition at the capital* — restore **and** surpass the grandeur of three generations ago. The
ceiling is honoured absolutely: recognition is **indirect & mediated** (down through rusui **Murase** and Lord
**Munenori**); the MC does **not** become a *hatamoto* and is **never** received by the shogun. His personal
ceiling stays **chief steward / *yōnin* — the lord's right hand** (grand *karō*/adoption vocabulary stays
aspirational narration only). The house's *banzuke* rank keeps climbing post-cap (personal vs house ascension
decoupled).

**The tier's mechanism:** the house's hands reach Edo **only inside the domain's body of work** — as the
daimyō's leading retainers, the Kurosawa help **staff & run the domain's Edo establishment** (the *rusui-yaku*),
and the MC equips, provisions, and burnishes the **daimyō's** biennial **sankin-kōtai** attendance; credit
accrues to the daimyō, and thence to the house. The Edo *yashiki* is the **domain's** — a corner of the
daimyō's compound, known to the house only through letters.

## T4.2 Main-quest beats (roadmap)

1. **The two conduits open (seeded late T3).** Genemon hands the MC a share of outfitting the **daimyō's**
   biennial journey (*sankin-kōtai*); the first sealed dispatch arrives from the domain's Edo residence (rusui
   **Murase**).
2. **The *meibutsu* reaches Edo.** Silk rides the procession up as tribute; a Nihonbashi *tonya* sends a single
   trial order (the silk's first capital-side buyer).
3. **The chart that omits you.** Murase forwards a popular ***mitate*/parody broadsheet** (sumo-rank vocabulary)
   on which a rival domain's product appears and the Kurosawa's does not — one stinging absence.
4. **Climbing the *banzuke*.** The silk first appears on a minor chart → climbs toward the attainable band
   (Maegashira/Komusubi) as the house's standing rises; the top ranks (Ōzeki/Yokozuna) are **structurally
   sealed** — the wall the truly powerful built, made the chart's literal geometry.
5. **The untouchable apex glimpsed. [MOTIF: rigged box] — terminus.** **Echizen-ya Sōbei**, the Edo factor
   laundering the skimmed silver, is glimpsed **once** (Murase forwards a dispatch; a manifest dies at his name)
   — you learn *who*, and that you **cannot touch him.** **The factor escapes** (the honoured incompleteness).
6. **The touring-inspector set-piece (capstone).** Word travels down the *kaidō* that **Junkenshi Kazami
   Saemon-no-jō** will survey the domain. The whole game's accumulated work becomes the answer to one exam; the
   **lord** faces the inspector, the house's record is read aloud, **the MC stands at the back.** **Won or
   dented**, never fought (a recoverable dent on neglect, **never a wipe**).
7. **The mediated commendation (the ending).** A favourable report travels up and becomes a documented
   merit-commendation entering an official register (the house's name in bakufu ink), delivered **down** through
   Murase and the lord. **One authored ending** (house restored & ranked) + **post-game free-play** (no reset);
   branches are in *how* you got there (allegiance / takeover route), not separate endings.

## T4.3 Antagonist arc — the Edo factor + the impartial test (roadmap)

- **Echizen-ya Sōbei (the Edo factor)** — the **untouchable apex** of the quota machine (the antagonist as
  *system*); glimpsed, named, **never reached.** Blocks *full justice* (the trail dies in Edo).
- **The Touring Inspector (Kazami Saemon-no-jō)** — **not a villain**; the antagonist-shaped **test** (the
  impartial judge whose survey validates or threatens everything the house built). Blocks *final recognition*.
  His secretary **Mabuchi** writes the report — the reachable human whose pen captures the house's worth
  (honestly informed, never bought).

## T4.4 Estate roster & buildings (roadmap) — E5 Restored-and-Surpassed

**Joins/escalates (roadmap):** **Rusui Murase Settsu-no-suke** (the MC's sole pen-pal proxy in the capital — the
single recurring T4 through-line face; the filter through whom every official contact passes); **Procession-
master Saburozaemon** (marshals the retinue; gatekeeps outfitting quality tiers); **Naoyuki** travels in the
retinue as the house's future face (the rivalry→brotherhood pays off as he reports back from Edo); the MC's
**own students** (Tokujirō now teaches under him; recruited origin friends + famine-orphan recruits "started
from zero"); **Jinpachi** fully integrated as a caravan/route contributor (the **[THREAD: Origin]** optional
emotional callback — the recovered family proud behind him; **no second mechanical payoff**).
**Buildings (roadmap):** a rebuilt grand *omoya* beyond the three-generations-ago original; a famous-*meibutsu*
workshop quarter; a school/training-hall where the MC now **teaches**; formal retainer barracks; the
restored-and-surpassed house seal — **the epilogue tableau.** **No reset** — E5 persists into post-game.

## T4.5 Endgame, succession & epilogue (roadmap)

**Succession.** Aging **Munenori**'s decline → heir **Naoyuki** comes into his own (the MC as right-hand); the
house's future is secured across a generation.

**The epilogue tableau** (a single warm, bittersweet image of everything built): the restored-and-surpassed
house seal; the reclaimed fields; the resettled **Kuzuhara**; the **named drowned**; the freed and
self-determining **Otsuru** (the lost-child truth is **always** known — spine-guaranteed at G6). **Two elements
are CONDITIONAL on the optional Origin O5 track (Q5):** the recovered family (incl. **Jinpachi**) proud behind
him, **and a true name — "Tahei."** A player who completed Origin sees the family and reclaims the name; a player
who **skipped** Origin still earns the **full restored-and-ranked ending and knows the Otsuru truth**, but the
tableau shows **no reclaimed family and no reclaimed name** — he remains "Tama," the borrowed name, and a life
still built by hand. **Partial justice, by design:** reachable culprits answered at their tiers; the truly
powerful (Iemasa, Echizen-ya Sōbei) walk. The win is **the house restored and ranked**, the rot's apex still out
of reach.

**Post-game long-tail (no reset; no decay-tax):** defend the top *banzuke* spot on the biennial sankin-kōtai
heartbeat (recoverable, never a decay-tax); optional grounded super-bosses; per-pillar mastery goals; the
estate-as-sandbox **teaching layer** re-homed onto **Tokujirō** + recruited origin friends.

---

## §5 cross-references & integration notes

- **Numbers belong in §4.** Every "result," "threshold," "result-recorded," and pillar-gate here is a **shape**;
  the per-tier required-pillar thresholds, seasonal judged-result formula, conversion weights, and big-number
  formatting are §4's job. **V2 adds these §4 surfaces §5 defers to:** the **rung-meter accrual curve** (FU6),
  the **three combat tracks + the character-level XP curve** (`MobDef.level · COMBAT_XP_K`; FU14/Q1), the
  **hybrid good/great/excellent gate thresholds** (FU10/Q7), the **E3 Prosperous cost row** (Q8), the **graded
  durability & armour bands** (FU17), the **satiety→combat throttle term** (FU16), and the **T2 cross-pillar
  combos** (FU20). §5 keeps only the *story shape*.
- **Rung copy belongs in §3.** T2's region-ladder rung wording, and all per-tier reveal cadences, are §3's job;
  §5 fixes only the *story shape* of each ladder. **§3 now also owns** the **staggered combat-reveal ladder**
  (FU12 — which UI/system surfaces at which rung) and the **per-rung curated-activity sets** (FU7); §5 fixes only
  the story shape of each ladder and the **phase-1 (climb) → phase-2 (grind) narrative arc.**
- **Dialogue choices are data, not scripting (Q34/FU22).** The Tama-vs-farmhand leaning and every multi-route
  antagonist resolution (Peaceful / Assertive / Partial) in §5 are authored as **intra-line dialogue choices** —
  a flat `choices[]`/`ChoiceId` list per node in `content/dialogue.ts`; deterministic; only the **chosen-flags
  persist**. The data/registry shape is §2/§6's job; §5 fixes only which beats offer a choice.
- **Authenticity pass — RESOLVED (2026-06-25).** The **Standing & Office** pillar kanji is set to **官威
  (*kan'i*)**, "authority of office" (coined 政威 rejected); the R7 rung title is **地方役 (*jikata-yaku*)**,
  "home-field bailiff" (the grander *jitō-dai* dropped); *michi-ban* (road-warden) is **diegetic-only** (an
  in-house role the estate confers, not an attested bakufu office); and the **T4** arc has the house **staff and
  run the *domain's* Edo establishment** (the *rusui-yaku* under the daimyō's *sankin-kōtai*), never its own.
  A routine follow-up confirm of humble *ashigaru*/household-tier titles vs aspirational narration may still
  run, but no design item is open.
- **Belief→cause tables are binding inputs to §2/§6** (the bestiary registry must contain **no belief-creatures**;
  yokai are INVESTIGATE-then-confront one-shots; exactly one ambiguous token at the weir-jizō find-spot).

## Items flagged for the human

1. **Authenticity pass — RESOLVED (2026-06-25).** The Standing & Office pillar kanji = **官威 (*kan'i*)**; R7 =
   **地方役 (*jikata-yaku*)** (home-field bailiff); *michi-ban* stays **diegetic-only**; **T4** = the house runs
   the *domain's* Edo conduit (*rusui-yaku* under the daimyō's *sankin-kōtai*), not its own. A routine confirm of
   humble *ashigaru*/household-tier title vocabulary vs aspirational narration may still run before T0–T2 dialogue
   is finalised, but no design item is open.
2. **Jinpachi's reunion kept un-stacked (confirm tone).** Authored as a **clean warm reunion**, deliberately
   **not** a third "debt-bondage / debt-machine-ate-a-person" arc beside Tahei's caravan and Otsuru's near-sale
   (the superseded father draft stacked exactly that; the verifier flagged it). Confirm this lighter framing —
   and whether his "presumed-dead → back-from-the-dead" return re-reads Oyuki at all.
3. **Otsuru / Osen name-proximity (RESOLVED).** The two post-town women previously near-rhymed; per human
   review the sweetheart was renamed to **Osen**, so they no longer blur. Authored with distinct roles/voices;
   no further rename needed.
4. **Kuzuhara has NO survivor / co-investigator character (CUT).** Per human review the stale fellow-survivor-
   who-recognizes-the-MC (the "Kiku"-type) is **cut entirely**: the MC has **no personal tie to Kuzuhara**.
   Kuzuhara is purely a struggling, depopulated upstream hamlet the player resettles as a T2 region project; the
   MC's origin is uncovered via the **dream → the Sawatari-juku family**, never via a Kuzuhara survivor. (The
   superseded spine's Ren/Kiku/Genza/Zenroku remain NOT canon and must not be revived.)
5. **T3/T4 are forward-authored only.** Per canon v1 scope (T3 stub = the **castle-town / Daikan first-contact**,
   Q24 — the old Porter-Guild stub is dropped; T4 roadmap). Confirm the chosen T3 stub node and that the
   parked/cut lists (**E4–E5** depth — **E3 Prosperous is now authored in v1** per Q8; Matagi / Pilgrimage /
   Scholars-network) match the human's intent before any post-v1 build.
6. **Name sweep applied (Q11/Q27/Q39 — confirm).** Renames carried through §5: field-lad **Mago → Tatsu**
   (off the antagonist **Magobei** + the porter's-knot homophone); herbalist **Obaa Sato → Obaa Mine** (off
   **Sayo**); clerk **Naozane → Yohachi** (off heir **Naoyuki**); touring inspector **Toyama → Kazami**; rusui
   **Konoe → Murase**. **Nihonbashi** stays as an allow-listed real place (Q12). Locked names unchanged; the
   §6.6 real-name lint guards against recurrence.

---
sections.s6:
# §6 — Tech Architecture & Data Model

This section specifies, concretely and leanly: the **toolchain**; the **pure-core boundary** and module
layout; the **GameState** shape (stored vs. computed — including the **three combat tracks** and the
sequential phase gate) and the `reduce`/`tick` contracts; the **one seeded RNG** (per-named-stream cursors +
stateless day-keyed weather); **content as data registries** (data-as-code, single source of truth —
including the **intra-line dialogue** data model and the **bounded per-skill cross-feed**); the **save model**
(a MULTI-BACKEND redundant save — IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage — with atomic writes, an
app-identity magic field, an additive backwards-compatible schema, and a monotonic-counter newest-wins
selector); the **renderer contract** (thin DOM, multi-screen UI, responsive, active-only); the **DEV play
API** (`window.__qa`); **accessibility basics**; and how all of this satisfies the four project conventions
(pure-core, one RNG, generate-don't-duplicate, playtest-by-code).

> **ADR note:** §6 is recorded as an **elaboration of D-013 (D-013a — tech architecture detail)** **and** —
> under the governing **D-022 (newest-block-wins)** — as the place that **implements the V2 tech decisions**:
> the multi-backend redundant save (Q37), the additive backwards-compatible schema (Q45), per-named-stream
> RNG cursors (Q2), the `Math.pow` ban (Q36), the bounded labour→combat verifier flip (Q6), the feature-rich
> data model incl. intra-line dialogue & `subEngines` (Q34), and the no-runtime-reveal-queue design rule
> (FU4). These are real reversals / new locks, not merely buildable detail (see §6.13).

---

## 6.1 Toolchain & build

**Stack (per D-013): Vite + TypeScript + Vitest, with ESLint + Prettier.** Committed `package.json` +
lockfile (the ad-hoc-toolchain weakness of the inspiration games is fixed by pinning everything). Output is
**fully static** — `vite build` → `dist/` → zipped and uploaded to itch.io, no server, no backend.

- **TypeScript: strict.** `"strict": true` plus `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`,
  `noImplicitOverride`. The core is typed end-to-end; content registries are typed data so the content
  verifier (§6.6) and the compiler share the work.
- **Vite** handles the dev server (HMR), TS compile, bundling, and cache-busting. The production bundle is a
  single static app; the DEV play API and any dev-only helpers are stripped via `import.meta.env.DEV`
  (dead-code-eliminated from the production build).
- **Vitest** unit-tests the pure core (it imports cleanly in Node because it has zero DOM/canvas/window
  references — see §6.2). Determinism tests assert that a fixed seed + a fixed intent/tick script produces a
  byte-identical `GameState` (snapshot or structural hash).
- **ESLint** enforces the architectural rules as lint, not just convention:
  - **no `Math.random()` anywhere in `src/core/`** (custom `no-restricted-syntax` / `no-restricted-globals`
    rule) — all randomness flows through `core/rng` (convention: *one RNG*);
  - **no `Math.pow` / `Math.exp` / `Math.log` / trig in `src/core/`** (whitelist `Math.sqrt`) — all
    growth-curve powers use **integer-power by repeated multiplication**, so saves replay **byte-identically
    across engines** and exported saves stay portable (kills the 1-ULP transcendental hole, Q36);
  - **no DOM/canvas/window/`document`/`localStorage`/`indexedDB`/`Date.now`/`performance.now` in
    `src/core/`** (a `no-restricted-imports` + `no-restricted-globals` boundary rule) — keeps the core pure,
    deterministic, and Node-testable (convention: *pure-core*). **One recorded exemption:** `src/persistence/`
    (NOT core) may call `Date.now` for the save-layer **timestamp** used only as the newest-wins **tiebreaker**
    (metadata, not game logic, FU2) — the deterministic core itself stays clock-free;
  - the renderer (`src/ui/`) may use the DOM but may **not** import from another screen's internals or
    mutate `GameState` directly (it only dispatches intents).
- **Prettier** for formatting; **lint-staged + the existing `.githooks/pre-commit`** keep each commit
  green and journaled (the hook already requires a `journal/` entry; `SKIP_JOURNAL=1` for trivial commits).

### `npm run verify` (the single gate)

One command must pass before any commit and in CI:

```
npm run verify  =  tsc --noEmit
               && eslint .
               && prettier --check .
               && vitest run
               && node scripts/verify-content.ts      # the content verifier (§6.6)
               && node scripts/gen-docs.ts --check     # generated docs are up to date (§6.6)
```

`gen:docs --check` regenerates the balance/content docs into a temp buffer and fails if they differ from
what's committed — this is how *generate-don't-duplicate* is enforced mechanically: you cannot land a data
change without regenerating its docs. `npm run gen:docs` (without `--check`) writes them.

**Scripts:** `dev` (Vite dev server), `build` (`vite build` → `dist/`), `preview`, `test`, `test:watch`,
`verify`, `gen:docs`, `lint`, `format`. `build:itch` = `build` + zip `dist/` for upload.

---

## 6.1.1 Bundled assets — the one acknowledged asset set (corrects "no asset pipeline")

The earlier "text + emoji + CSS, **no asset pipeline**" framing is **corrected**: v1 ships **one small,
curated, fully-bundled asset set** (everything self-hosted in `dist/`, works offline and under itch's
relative-base iframe):

- **Self-hosted OFL fonts (Q52).** The display/body typefaces are SIL **Open Font License** fonts,
  **self-hosted** (woff2 committed into the repo / bundled by Vite) — **no Google dynamic subsetting** (which
  breaks offline play and itch's relative base URL). The **OFL license text is bundled** alongside them.
- **A small curated audio set (Q50).** A light ambient bed + UI/event SFX, made of **synthesized Web Audio**
  (procedural, code-only) plus a few **original / CC0** samples — the ONE acknowledged sample set. Bundled
  into `dist/`; mute toggle per §6.11.
- **Inline SVG for load-bearing period motifs (Q38).** Pillar / season / rank / rarity marks that must be
  **identical across OSes** are **inline SVG** (not emoji); **emoji stay cosmetic-only**.
- **Commit-SHA build stamp (Q54).** The build injects the current **commit SHA** + build date as a constant,
  surfaced on the About/Credits screen (§6.5 / §2.21) for reproducible bug reports.

All of this is plain static output — no server, no runtime fetch — consistent with the static-deploy canon
(§6.1, §6.8).

---

## 6.2 The pure-core boundary

**The single most valuable architectural rule (CLAUDE.md conventions): all game logic — rules, state,
math — lives in a pure core with ZERO DOM / canvas / window imports.** The renderer consumes the core as
plain data. This makes the core deterministic, unit-testable in Node, and headlessly drivable.

```
src/
  core/        ← PURE. No DOM/canvas/window. The whole game's logic & data.
  ui/          ← thin DOM renderer. Reads GameState, dispatches intents. No game logic.
  app/         ← composition root: wires core ↔ ui ↔ persistence; the tick loop (ACCUMULATES the fractional
                 dtTicks remainder, hands only WHOLE-INTEGER ticks to core.tick; drives auto-resolve combat +
                 auto-repeat labour while the tab is active — the "leave it running" feel, still active-only);
                 __qa in DEV.
  persistence/ ← MULTI-BACKEND (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage) + atomic write + app-identity magic
                 field + monotonic-counter newest-wins selector + the additive-schema migration chain &
                 raw-backup. (Side-effectful; never imported BY core.)
scripts/       ← gen-docs.ts, verify-content.ts (Node entry points; import core data only).
```

**Dependency rule (one-directional):** `ui`, `app`, `persistence`, and `scripts` may import from `core`;
**`core` imports from none of them.** `core` exports only pure functions and data and the `GameState` type.
The lint boundary rule (§6.1) makes a violation a build failure, not a code-review catch.

### `core/` module layout

| Module | Responsibility | Pure? |
|---|---|---|
| `core/state` | The `GameState` type, `createInitialState(seed)`, and the **stored vs. computed** split (§6.4). | yes |
| `core/intents` | The typed `Intent` union (every player verb) + `reduce(state, intent) -> state` — the action reducer. | yes |
| `core/step` | `tick(state, dtTicks) -> state` — the deterministic clock advance + the per-tick / per-day / per-week / per-season scheduler. | yes |
| `core/rng` | The **one** seeded RNG: pure `next(rng, stream) -> [value, rng']`, helpers (`int`, `chance`, `pick`, `weighted`). Persisted as **per-named-stream cursors** `{ seed, cursors:{ combat, loot, seasonal, worldgen } }` (NOT a single counter, NOT child-RNGs-by-splitting; §6.7). Plus a pure **stateless** day-keyed helper `deriveDayKeyed(seed,'weather',day)` for weather/lunar — NOT a persisted cursor (§6.7.1). | yes |
| `core/combat` | The deterministic, fixed-step auto-battler (per-weapon `baseSpeed`; seeded hit/crit/block/damage/loot). Feeds **combat-XP → `character.level`** (Q1/FU14); reads a per-mob **`MobDef.level`** (FU15); applies **graded durability bands** on `attackPower`/`defense` (FU17) and the **satiety throttle** (`satietyRate` on `attackPower`, FU16); supports a **clean retreat** (Q16); honours the staggered reveal of stance / ability / item slots (FU12) and a **bigger weapon roster** with archetype params + a signature ability (FU13); persists a `CombatEncounterState` for **save-resume** (Q34). Stepped by `core/step`. | yes |
| `core/economy` | Producers, costs, resource flows (the *koku*/coin spine; the capped Estate & Wealth sub-engines: land / treasury / trade incl. the silk *meibutsu*, trade ≤⅓-capped per canon §D). | yes |
| `core/skills` | Per-skill XP curves, per-event caps, visibility thresholds, milestone web; each skill (labour + combat) carries a **per-skill PERKS track** (~2–8 perks / small flat combat bonuses, unlocked by leveling it). This is the **bounded labour→combat channel** — the real bound is **incremental skill unlock** (skills reveal per rung/tier), not a global cap; **conditioning stays the ZERO-stat enablement gate**, orthogonal to perks. | yes |
| `core/rewards` | The universal **rewards/unlock bus** — `applyRewards(state, rewards) -> state` — the one funnel through which dialogue, quests, thresholds, and combat grant items/xp/coin/locations/recipes/quests/**flags & unlocks**, and emit diegetic log lines. | yes |
| `core/unlock` | Predicate evaluation for the UI-reveal engine: each panel/screen/tab/row/area is data with an unlock predicate over `GameState`; `isUnlocked(state, id)` and `visibleSurfaces(state)`. **Reveal staggering is a DESIGN property of the authored unlock schedule** (one-at-a-time by construction) — there is **NO** stored runtime reveal-queue; genuine multi-element single-feature reveals are bespoke one-offs designed per case (FU4). | yes |
| `core/influence` | The four House-Influence pillars (Arms / Estate & Wealth / Standing & Office / Name & Honour), achievement-jump + seasonal judged-result accrual (new-high-water-mark, up-only + per-pillar recoverable dents). The phase-2 **HYBRID good/great/excellent per-pillar-per-tier gate** (good in ALL revealed pillars, great in 2–3, excellent in 1–2; T0 2-pillar special; **NO overflow**); pillar **DEEDS gated to phase 2** (FU7); the nested `estateWealth.subEngines{land,treasury,trade}` clamp (trade ≤⅓); **cross-pillar combos computed POST the trade-clamp** (canon §D/§12). | yes |
| `core/ranks` | The **per-rung-reset rung-meter** + the promotion **AND-gate** (meter ≥ that rung's threshold AND story flags met; surfaces "awaiting X" when one lags) + the **sequential phase gate** per tier: phase 1 (climb the rungs via curated per-rung activities, 1-to-many) → phase 2 (the estate-influence / pillar grind unlocks after the final/capstone rung). The phase marker is **derivable from the current rung** — no stored phase flag. | yes |
| `core/content` | The **data registries** (one module per content type; §6.5) + the registry index. Data-as-code. | yes |
| `core/log` | The event/story log model (append, cap, severity/colour tag) — data only; the renderer paints it. | yes |
| `core/selectors` | Derived/computed reads (current production rates, effective stats, what's unlocked, current tier). **Pure functions of `GameState`; nothing stored.** | yes |

**Public surface of `core`:** the `GameState` type, `createInitialState`, `reduce`, `tick`, the selectors,
the registries, and the RNG helpers. **Nothing else mutates state.** Everything is immutable-in/immutable-out
(structural sharing; never mutate the input `state`).

---

## 6.3 The two contracts: `reduce` and `tick`

Two pure functions are the *only* ways state changes. Both are deterministic given `(state, input)` because
all randomness is carried inside `state.rng` (§6.7).

```ts
// Every player action is a typed intent. Discriminated union.
type Intent =
  | { type: 'rake_rice' }
  | { type: 'do_activity'; activityId: ActivityId }   // farm/forage/woodcut/fish/craft/...
  | { type: 'travel'; areaId: AreaId }
  | { type: 'buy_producer'; producerId: ProducerId }
  | { type: 'equip'; itemId: ItemId; slot: EquipSlot }
  | { type: 'set_stance'; stanceId: StanceId }
  | { type: 'use_item'; itemId: ItemId }
  | { type: 'combat_action'; action: CombatAction }   // ability/item/retreat — strategic, low-APM
  | { type: 'advance_dialogue'; lineId: DialogueLineId; choiceId?: ChoiceId }  // intra-line branch: a chosen choiceId writes only CHOSEN-flags (locksLineIds[]/flags), deterministic, save-light (FU22)
  | { type: 'accept_quest'; questId: QuestId }
  | { type: 'read_scroll'; scrollId: ScrollId }       // diegetic feature unlock (costs in-game time)
  | { type: 'set_allegiance'; lean: number }          // Tama ↔ farmhand, continuous, re-swingable
  // ...one variant per verb; the renderer NEVER mutates state, it only dispatches these.

// The action reducer: a player verb → new state. Pure; deterministic.
function reduce(state: GameState, intent: Intent): GameState;

// The clock advance: simulate dtTicks of abstract game-time. Pure; deterministic.
// dtTicks is ABSTRACT (active-play ticks) AND a WHOLE INTEGER — the app loop accumulates
// the fractional remainder and only ever hands whole ticks to core. Never wall-clock.
// Active-only, no offline (canon §H).
function tick(state: GameState, dtTicks: number): GameState;
```

**`reduce`** validates the intent against current state (e.g. enough *koku*, area reachable, rung high
enough), applies the change, runs any triggered rewards through `core/rewards`, and re-checks unlock and
tier-threshold predicates so newly-earned surfaces flip to unlocked and push their diegetic log line. An
illegal intent is a no-op (returns the same state) plus an optional rejection note — never a throw.

**`tick`** advances the abstract clock by `dtTicks` and runs the scheduler in `core/step`:
- **per-tick:** combat sub-step (the auto-battler) including **auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour** while
  the tab is active (the "leave it running, check progress" feel — still strictly active-only, FU23),
  satiety/energy drain (soft — slows, never hard-blocks), active-activity progress, auto-producer output
  (T3+ only).
- **per-day / per-week:** vendor restocks, food rot/ferment, the market-saturation damper recovering, and the
  **`deriveDayKeyed` weather / festival resolution** (the mechanical **±10%** modifier, derived stateless from
  `seed + day` — never stored; §6.7.1).
- **per-season:** harvest resolution, the **seasonal judged Influence appraisal** (runs **every season —
  4×/in-game year**, the autumn harvest the headline; it **raises a pillar only on a new high-water mark
  (up-only)**, never a per-season maintenance trickle, per canon §D and PRD §1.6.2). **Per FU7 (sequential
  per-tier): pillar deeds accrue ONLY in phase 2** — after the tier's final/capstone rung; during the phase-1
  rung-climb the appraisal records nothing toward the pillars. Weather/festival events (the day-keyed ±10%)
  modulate the judged result.

`dtTicks` is computed by the **app-layer** loop from real elapsed time *while the tab is active*; the loop
**accumulates the fractional remainder** and hands only **whole-integer** ticks to the pure `tick` (so the
integer tick stream is itself deterministic, Q2/FU3). The core never reads a clock. **Active-only is enforced
structurally:** there is no offline-accrual code path — on load, the world resumes exactly where it was saved
(no "while you were away").

> **Determinism guarantee (testable):** `replay(initialState, [intents…], [tickScript…])` produces an
> identical `GameState` every run. This is asserted in Vitest and is what makes the DEV play API (§6.9) a
> regression harness for pacing and unlock timing.

---

## 6.4 GameState — stored vs. computed

`GameState` is a single immutable-ish tree reduced by `reduce`/`tick`. The cardinal rule (per the architecture
sketch and the *save-minimal* canon): **store only what is NOT derivable; recompute everything derivable on
read.** Derived values (effective stats, current production/sec, what's unlocked, current tier, time-to-next)
are **computed by `core/selectors`, never stored** — so they can never go stale and never need a migration.

### Stored (non-derivable) — the persisted surface

```ts
interface GameState {
  schemaVersion: number;            // for ordered migrations (§6.8)
  meta: { app: 'kami-kakushi'; saveCounter: number; savedAt?: number };  // save-layer identity/arbitration (§6.8.1): magic field + monotonic newest-wins counter + Date.now TIEBREAKER (the documented core-lint exemption)
  rng: { seed: number; cursors: { combat: number; loot: number; seasonal: number; worldgen: number } };  // per-named-stream cursors — persisted (§6.7); weather/lunar NOT stored (derived day-keyed, §6.7.1)
  clock: { tick: number; day: number; season: Season; year: number };  // abstract time
  currentArea: AreaId;                            // where the player IS now (set by the `travel` intent) — non-derivable, persisted (§2.19 "current location")
  resources: Record<ResourceId, number>;         // koku, coin(mon), wood, fish, materials…
  producers: Record<ProducerId, number>;         // owned counts (late-game only)
  market: { saturation: Record<ResourceId, number> };  // the ONLY non-derivable economy/world-sim state; weather/lunar are DERIVED (day-keyed, §6.7.1), belief-beasts are content
  skills: Record<SkillId, { xp: number }>;       // total xp per skill; levels DERIVED
  character: { hp: number; satiety: number; attributePoints: number;
               level: number; combatXp: number;            // combat-fed track (Q1/FU14); level≥1; hpMax/attr/satietyMax DERIVED off level (§4, §6.4.1)
               attributes: Record<AttrId, number> };  // base attrs stored; effective DERIVED
  inventory: Record<ItemId, number>;             // counts (quality folded into the item key)
  equipment: Partial<Record<EquipSlot, { equipDefId: EquipDefId; durability: number; qualityTier: QualityTier }>>;  // per-slot EquipState (§2.10) — durability/quality persist HERE, not in the counts-only inventory; durability BAND is DERIVED from the stored integer (§4)
  influence: Record<PillarId, { value: number; highWater: number; dent: Dent | null;
               subEngines?: { land: SubEngineState; treasury: SubEngineState; trade: SubEngineState } }>;  // 4 pillars; value + high-water + the (≤1) active recoverable dent (§4.2.4); the estateWealth entry additionally NESTS subEngines (land/treasury/trade, each value+highWater) so the trade-≤⅓ clamp has real storage & combos compute against the CLAMPED value (§2.19)
  tier: TierId;                                   // current macro tier T0..T4 (DERIVED-checkable, stored for clarity)
  ranks: Record<TierId, { estateService: number; combatRank: number; rung: RankId }>;  // per-tier PER-RUNG-RESET rung-meters: estateService (labour) + combatRank (martial; renamed from 'Combat Standing' per Q9; fed by per-rung CURATED activities, NOT raw kills/XP). Phase-1/phase-2 is DERIVABLE from `rung` (no stored phase flag)
  reputation: Record<FactionNodeId, number>;     // village per-node meters; origin ties as milestones
  allegiance: number;                            // Tama ↔ farmhand lean, continuous
  flags: Set<FlagId>;                            // story/finished/one-shot flags + intra-line dialogue CHOSEN-flags (§6.5 dialogue.ts) (serialized as array)
  unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>;                       // panels/screens/areas the player has earned — the ONLY reveal state (NO revealQueue; see callout below)
  quests: Record<QuestId, { status: QuestStatus; step: number }>;
  counts: Record<CountId, number>;               // kills, clears, harvests — drive quest advancement & bestiary tallies (NOT a separate player "achievements" feature; pillar achievement-JUMPS are recognized deeds, §2.16, not these raw counts)
  effects: ActiveEffect[];                        // active buffs/injuries with remaining duration
  combat?: CombatEncounterState;                  // present only while a fight is live; NON-derivable mid-fight (consumed RNG, current HP/positions/statuses cannot be recomputed) — stored so a save resumes the exact encounter; cleared when the fight ends. ADDITIVE OPTIONAL (added at its milestone, not in the M0 skeleton)
  log: LogEntry[];                                // capped event/story log
  settings: { textScale: number; colourblindMode: boolean; reducedMotion: boolean; muted: boolean };
}
```

> **NO `revealQueue` field.** There is deliberately **no** `revealQueue` (or any runtime reveal-buffer) in
> `GameState`. `unlocked: Set<SurfaceId>` is the **only** reveal state; reveal **staggering** is a **design
> property of the authored unlock schedule** (built one-at-a-time by construction), **not** stored runtime
> state — this supersedes Q17's "deterministic one-per-beat queue" (FU4).

> **Additive optional fields (M0 skeleton vs. later milestones).** Only `{ hp, satiety, attributePoints,
> character.level (=1) }` ship in the **M0** skeleton (FU5). `combat` (CombatEncounterState),
> `estateWealth.subEngines`, and the dialogue chosen-flags are **additive optional** fields added at their
> milestone (M3 / M5), defaulting to absent on older saves (Q45/FU5; §6.8.2).

### Computed (never stored) — examples from `core/selectors`

`effectiveStats(state)` (base + additive + multiplier layers + equipment + milestones + **per-skill perks**);
`satietyMax(state)` (base + per-level growth from `character.level`, Q47); `combatPower(state)` (effective
`attackPower` **including** the bounded per-skill perks, the satiety throttle, and the durability band);
`durabilityBand(state, slot)` (the 4-band lookup from the stored durability integer, §4); `productionPerTick(state)`;
`unlockedSurfaces(state)`; `currentTier(state)` and `tierThresholdProgress(state)`; `gateProfile(state)` (the
good/great/excellent distribution across the **revealed** pillars, Q7/FU10); `skillLevel(skillId, state)`
(from xp + curve); `timeToNextGoal(state)` (for the greyed next-purchase). These are pure, cheap, memoizable
per-snapshot, and **excluded from the save**.

> **Why this split matters:** it keeps the save tiny and forward-compatible (you only ever migrate
> non-derivable fields), makes the renderer a pure function of state, and means a balance retune (a curve
> change) instantly reflows every derived number with **no migration** — only stored facts ever migrate.
> **And new stored facts are added as OPTIONAL fields with defaults (never removed/repurposed), so most
> schema growth needs no migration at all** (the additive backwards-compatible schema, Q45/FU5; §6.8.2).

---

### 6.4.1 Three combat tracks & the sequential per-tier phase gate (data-model view)

The FU14 de-conflation is explicit at the data level — **three independent tracks, each summing on its own,
never cross-feeding** (the old "Combat Level = a Combat-Deeds pool" that fused all three is **deleted**):

| Track | Stored in | Fed by | One kill writes | Drives |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 · Character (combat) level** | `character.level` + `character.combatXp` | kills / combat-XP = `MobDef.level × COMBAT_XP_K` | **combat-XP only** | `hpMax = 40 + 8·level`, `satietyMax` (derived: base + per-level growth), **+1 attribute point / 2 levels** (§4) |
| **2 · Arms pillar (武威 *bu-i*)** | `influence['arms']` | recognised **deeds** (PHASE-2 only) | **nothing** | House Influence — 1 of 4 pillars at the hybrid gate |
| **3 · Combat Rank rung-meter** | `ranks[tier].combatRank` | per-rung **curated** activities (NOT raw kills) | **nothing directly** | martial **rung** promotions in **phase 1** |

- **"Combat Rank"** is the project-wide term (Q9) for track 3; **"Standing"** now means the **官威 / Office**
  pillar **only**.
- **Sequential phase gate (FU7).** Per tier: **Phase 1** = climb the rungs (the rung-meter + story AND-gate) →
  **Phase 2** = the estate-influence / pillar grind, which **unlocks only after the tier's final/capstone
  rung** (T0=R7, T1=V7, T2=G7). **Pillar deeds accrue in phase 2 only.** The **phase is DERIVABLE from the
  current rung** — there is **no stored phase flag**.
- M0 ships only **track 1's skeleton** (`character.level = 1`); the Arms pillar and the rung-meter arrive at
  their milestones (§6.8.2).

## 6.5 Content as data registries (data-as-code, single source of truth)

**All content is authored as plain, typed TypeScript data in `core/content`, one module per type** — consumed
by the pure core, never co-located with DOM or behaviour. This inverts both inspiration games' mistake of
binding data to the renderer, and it is the backbone of *generate-don't-duplicate*.

| Registry module | Holds | Keyed by |
|---|---|---|
| `content/resources.ts` | resources (koku, coin, wood, fish, materials…) + display/emoji + caps | `ResourceId` |
| `content/activities.ts` | jobs/labour nodes (farm/forage/woodcut/fish/craft) — yields, skill, season/area gates; **per-rung CURATED activity sets** (tagged by rung) that feed the rung-meter, authored **separately** from the pillar-deed inventory | `ActivityId` |
| `content/producers.ts` | late-game auto-producers — cost curve refs, output, unlock predicate | `ProducerId` |
| `content/skills.ts` | skills — xp curve refs, per-event cap, visibility threshold, milestones; a **per-skill PERKS track** (~2–8 perks / small flat combat bonuses, gated to skill levels) — the bounded labour→combat channel (the verifier asserts **small** per-perk magnitude, not ==0 and not a global cap) | `SkillId` |
| `content/items.ts` | items/equipment/consumables — slots, stats, rarity, quality rules; the **weapon roster** (~9–10 across v1: T0 **starts with 1**, then **+2 / +3 / +4** across T0/T1/T2), each weapon carrying **archetype params** (`baseSpeed` / `reach` / `targetCount`) + a **signature ability**, anchored by 3 archetype **lines** (spear / sword / staff); graded **durability bands** on `attackPower` (§4) | `ItemId` |
| `content/recipes.ts` | crafting — inputs, station tier, quality-from-skill rules, disassembly | `RecipeId` |
| `content/enemies.ts` | the **grounded** bestiary (boars/wolves/monkeys/bandits/ronin/smugglers — NO belief-creatures in spawn tables, canon §E) — stats, `attackSpeed`, an explicit per-mob **`level`** (`MobDef.level`, defaults ~ the dangerRing's expected character-level; feeds on-kill XP, §4.6.5), loot tables | `EnemyId` |
| `content/world.ts` | world-sim rules — `SeasonRules` / `Festival` / `WeatherHazard`: the mechanical **±10%** day-keyed modifiers (data-as-code; resolved statelessly via `deriveDayKeyed`, §6.7.1) | (named) |
| `content/beliefBeasts.ts` | the **belief-creatures** (kept **OUT** of grindable spawn tables; `enemies.ts` stays grounded-only, canon §E) — lore/appearance data | `BeliefBeastId` |
| `content/areas.ts` | the full per-tier maps (T0–T2 in v1) — nodes, travel edges, conditioning gates, faction | `AreaId` |
| `content/dialogue.ts` | dialogue lines — text, display conditions, the rewards object, branch locks; **intra-line branching as flat DATA**: `choices[]` + `ChoiceId` with `locksLineIds[]` / flags effects (not scripting; deterministic; **only chosen-flags persist**, FU22) | `DialogueLineId` |
| `content/quests.ts` | quests — sequential steps, advance events, rewards (open-ended, non-waypoint); **NO quest-type budget** — PEST/HUNT/CLEAR/DEFEND is just the **T0 starting set**, not a cap; author whatever quests fit each stage, more/interesting welcome at later tiers (Q23) | `QuestId` |
| `content/scrolls.ts` | lore scrolls — in-game-time cost, the subsystem they unlock | `ScrollId` |
| `content/surfaces.ts` | every panel / screen / tab / row / button — its **unlock predicate** + which screen it lives on (drives the UI-reveal engine and multi-screen nav); incl. **Crafting** and **Quests** as **top-level tabs** (Q10) and the **About / Credits** surface (authorship, the commit-SHA build stamp, font/audio attributions, license note; §2.21 / §6.1.1) | `SurfaceId` |
| `content/ranks.ts` | the **fresh rank ladder PER TIER** (T0/T1/T2 enumerated for v1) — rung, track (labour/combat/mixed), earn-condition, unlock; each rung carries its **rung-meter threshold + the story AND-gate**, and the rungs encode the **combat-reveal ladder** (R3 weapon + auto-resolve loop + retreat → R4 durability bands → R5 stance → first weapon-L10 ability/item slots → 2nd line @T1 / 3rd @T2) | `RankId` |
| `content/influence.ts` | the four pillars + the **per-pillar-per-tier good / great / excellent gate-band triples** (the V2 overhaul, balanced against the fixed §4.2.1 deed inventory — NOT simple ratios; values cross-ref §4) + accrual **deeds** (gated to **phase 2**, FU7) + the **cross-pillar combos** (computed POST the trade-≤⅓ clamp, landing on a third pillar, **excluded** from the gate-threshold check) | `PillarId` / `DeedId` |
| `content/effects.ts` | buffs/injuries/status — magnitude, duration, stacking | `EffectId` |
| `content/balance.ts` | shared curve/constant definitions — the *single* home for tunables; §4 sets the values: cost growth, xp scaling, the **rung-meter thresholds**, the **good/great/excellent gate bands**, **per-skill perk magnitudes**, the **durability bands** (75+/50+/1+/0 → 1.0/0.9/0.75/0.55), the **satiety throttle** (flat ≥~0.7 → ~0.5 floor), **weather ±10%**, the **combat-XP→level curve**, and `MobDef.level` defaults — **all integer-pow only** (no `Math.pow`) | (named) |

**Rewards are one shape everywhere.** Dialogue, quests, gathering thresholds, and combat all grant the same
`Rewards` object (`{ items?, xp?, resources?, unlocks?, areas?, recipes?, quests?, flags?, dialogues?, pillarDeltas? }`),
funnelled through `core/rewards.applyRewards`. Story flags and UI reveals ride the *same* bus — so "story" and
"UI growth" are one dependency graph (a reveal reads as plot, not menu growth), exactly as §1/§3 require.
**`pillarDeltas` (deed accrual) is PHASE-2-gated (FU7):** a deed grants pillar value only **after** the tier's
final rung — never during the phase-1 rung-climb. The **dialogue `choices[]` effects** (chosen-flags /
`locksLineIds`) ride this **same** bus.

## 6.6 The content verifier + generated docs (generate-don't-duplicate)

**Content verifier** (`scripts/verify-content.ts`, run in `npm run verify`): cross-checks every id/reference
across all registries (a recipe's inputs exist; an area's travel edges resolve; a quest's reward items exist;
a rank's unlock surface exists; every `SurfaceId` referenced by a screen is registered; no orphan ids). It
also asserts the **canon invariants** as machine checks so they cannot silently rot:

- no `EnemyId` in any spawn/population table is tagged `belief-creature` (canon §E);
- the **trade** sub-engine of Estate & Wealth is capped at **≤⅓** of that pillar (canon §D) — and **holds even
  after cross-pillar combos** (combos are computed POST the trade-clamp, land on a third pillar, and are
  EXCLUDED from gate-threshold checks; the verifier proves trade can never breach ⅓ via a combo, §6.6.1);
- exactly **≤1** residual-ambiguity token exists across content (canon §A);
- no Influence path is a passive per-tick trickle or flat per-action increment (accrual is jump/judged only);
- **conditioning grants ZERO combat stat / training-rate bonus** (it stays the zero-stat enablement gate) —
  BUT each **per-skill perk** grants a **small bounded** combat bonus, and the verifier asserts each perk's
  **magnitude is small** (a per-perk magnitude check) — **NOT ==0** and **NOT a single global ≤CAP** (the old
  absolute no-cross-feed wall is removed, Q6/FU8; §6.6.1);
- **macron / no-plain-ASCII-romaji lint:** display/name strings use proper-Hepburn **macrons** (Tōkichi,
  Yagōemon, Jūbei, *gōshi*, *rōnin*, *kyō-masu*) — the verifier flags plain-ASCII romaji that should carry a
  macron, so no ASCII-slip can land in shipped text (canon §H); its examples track the **"Combat Standing" →
  "Combat Rank"** rename (Q9). **Allow-list:** naturalized English exonyms — *shogun, ronin, yokai, samurai,
  Osaka, daimyō* (written with its macron) — are exempt; the **fictional/place allow-list** additionally
  carries **Nihonbashi** (Q12, an explicitly allow-listed real place). Cross-linked with the **real-name
  denylist** (§6.6.1).

**Generated docs** (`scripts/gen-docs.ts`): a Node entry that imports the same registries the game runs on and
writes balance/content tables into `docs/` (e.g. `docs/balance/curves.md`, `docs/content/bestiary.md`,
`docs/content/areas.md`, `docs/content/ranks.md`, `docs/content/influence.md`). Run with `--check` in
`verify` to fail the build if the committed docs drift from the data. This is the convention *generate, don't
duplicate* made literal: balance/content tables are **never hand-maintained twice** — they are a build
artifact of the one source of truth. Any number **hand-typed** into prose that is actually derived is tagged
**"illustrative — see generated"** so a hand value can't silently drift from the generated table (Q41).

---

### 6.6.1 V2 verifier invariants

The V2 model adds machine checks (collected here; run inside `verify-content.ts`) so the new balance shape
can't silently rot:

- **Gate-monotonicity / ceiling.** No rung or tier requires **more** than its tier can grant — each rung-meter
  target and each pillar gate-band is **reachable** from that tier's eligible activities/deeds (no impossible
  wall).
- **Accrual tie-out.** The **phase-2 deed inventory** sums to the tier's gate share **within tolerance** (the
  deeds available actually add up to the good/great/excellent thresholds they're back-solved against, §4.8).
- **Per-rung-meter reachability.** Every rung's rung-meter threshold = (≥30-min floor × that rung's eligible
  curated-activity rate) and is **achievable** from the rung's tagged activities (the rung-meter law, §4; FU6).
- **Per-skill-perk magnitude (FLIPPED).** Conditioning is asserted to grant **ZERO** combat stat/rate; each
  per-skill perk is asserted to grant a **small bounded** bonus — **not ==0, not a global cap** (Q6/FU8). The
  two channels stay **orthogonal** (a perk can't back-door past conditioning's weak→capable gate).
- **Hybrid gate-distribution.** The phase-2 tier-gate is **good in ALL revealed pillars · great in 2–3 ·
  excellent in 1–2**, with the **T0 two-pillar special case** (good in both + excellent in one — no phantom
  3rd) and **NO overflow-substitution**; the check only ever tests the **revealed** pillar set (T0=2
  Arms+Estate / T1=3 +Office / T2=3–4), and **combos are excluded** so a combo can never satisfy a required
  pillar (Q7/FU10/FU20).
- **Trade ≤⅓ post-combo proof.** Trade stays ≤⅓ of Estate & Wealth **even after** combos (computed post-clamp,
  on a third pillar, excluded from gate checks) — verifier-proven a combo can never breach ⅓ (Q22/FU20).
- **Real-name denylist lint.** Flags real surnames / places (Toyama, Konoe, real daimyō / real place names) in
  any display string, maintained alongside the macron lint; **allow-list** = naturalized exonyms + the
  invented set (Asagiri, Sawatari-juku, Kuzuhara…) + the explicitly allow-listed **Nihonbashi**
  (Q28/Q27/Q39/Q12). Guards the V2 renames (Toyama→Kazami, Konoe→Murase, Mago→Tatsu, Naozane→Yohachi, Obaa
  Sato→Obaa Mine) against recurrence.

---

## 6.7 The one seeded RNG — per-named-stream cursors

**One seeded RNG for the entire game** (canon §H, convention *one RNG*). A small, fast, well-distributed
generator — **splitmix64** (LOCKED, §6.13 #2), seeded at new-game from a stored seed.

- **It is part of `GameState`** as **per-named-stream cursors**: `state.rng = { seed, cursors: { combat, loot,
  seasonal, worldgen } }` — one **monotonic cursor per named sub-stream**, all rooted in the one `seed`. This
  **replaces** the old single `{ seed, counter }` *and* the "derive child RNGs by splitting" model: each
  subsystem advances its **own** cursor, so combat rolls and loot rolls and seasonal events stay independent
  **and** reproducible without coupling (Q2/FU3). The whole `rng` is **saved & loaded**, so resumed games
  reproduce exactly.
- The API is **pure**: `next(rng, stream) -> [n, rng']` returns the value *and* the advanced RNG (with that
  named cursor bumped); helpers (`rngInt`, `rngChance`, `rngPick`, `rngWeighted`) take the stream name and
  thread the new RNG back into `GameState` via `reduce`/`tick`. Combat, loot, and dialogue flavour each draw
  from their named stream.
- **Weather / lunar / festivals are NOT a persisted cursor.** They are derived **statelessly** from `seed +
  day` via `deriveDayKeyed` (§6.7.1) — nothing weather-related is stored, and the same day always reproduces
  the same modifier.
- **`Math.random()` is banned in `core/` by lint** (§6.1) — there is no second, hidden source of randomness —
  and **`Math.pow`/`exp`/`log`/trig are banned too** (integer-pow only; §6.1) so every draw and every curve is
  byte-identical across engines.

> This is what lets a saved fight resume identically, lets the DEV play API force a rare loot/crit outcome by
> seed, and lets Vitest assert byte-identical replays — across browsers and across exported/imported saves.

---

### 6.7.1 Stateless day-keyed derivations (weather / lunar / festivals)

`deriveDayKeyed(seed, channel, day) -> value` is a **pure, stateless** helper (NOT a persisted cursor): it
hashes `seed + channel + day` through splitmix64 to produce that day's value. Weather, lunar phase, and
festival modifiers — the mechanical **±10%** band (canon §16; §4) — are reproduced **on demand** from `seed +
day`, so:

- **nothing weather-related is stored** (the day index in `clock` is enough to recompute it);
- **replays stay byte-identical** (the same day always derives the same value, regardless of when it is asked);
- it never advances a cursor, so it can't desync the combat / loot / seasonal streams.

The **only** world-sim state that *is* persisted is **market saturation** (`state.market.saturation`, §6.4) —
the one quantity that genuinely accumulates and is not a pure function of `seed + day`. Belief-beasts are
**content** (`content/beliefBeasts.ts`, §6.5), not state.

---

## 6.8 Save model — multi-backend redundant save, versioned, additive-schema

Per **D-013 / canon §H** *and the governing V2 decisions (Q37/Q44/Q45/Q46, FU1/FU2)*: robust, durable,
static-friendly, **no backend** — but **redundant across every storage backend the browser offers**, so an
itch cross-origin-iframe partition or a single backend's eviction can't lose the save.

- **Multi-backend, redundant (Q37/FU1).** One **backend abstraction** wraps **IndexedDB (primary)** +
  **localStorage** + **sessionStorage**. On save, write the same blob **atomically to every available
  backend**; on load, read **all** backends and pick the **newest** (§6.8.1). The **FULL layer is built in
  M0** (abstraction + atomic write + magic field + newest-wins selector); rich per-system fields are added
  **additively** later (FU1).
- **Single autosave slot** (canon: *single autosave*) — the redundancy is across **backends**, not multiple
  slots.
- **Export / import: base64 to a text file (Q37).** The same serialized save is base64-encoded for copy-paste
  / file export and import — the player's portable backup (and a hand-off path for QA). Import validates (magic
  field + schemaVersion) + migrates. This manual backstop is **periodically nudged** as a first-class backup
  (the "export a backup" posture) so a silent autosave loss is caught.
- **Persist only non-derivable state (§6.4).** The save is the **stored** surface of `GameState`:
  schemaVersion, the **save-layer metadata** (magic field `app:'kami-kakushi'`, monotonic save-counter,
  timestamp tiebreaker), the RNG **cursors** (`{ seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} }`, NOT
  seed+counter), clock, current area, resources, producer counts, **market saturation**, skill xp, base
  attributes, **`character.level` + `combatXp`**, current vitals (hp / satiety / attribute-points), inventory,
  equipment, influence pillars (value + high-water + dents, incl. `estateWealth.subEngines`), stored tier, the
  per-rung-reset **rung-meter values** (`estateService` + `combatRank`), reputation, allegiance, flags (incl.
  dialogue chosen-flags), unlocked surfaces, quest status, counts, the live combat encounter (present only
  while a fight is live), the capped event log, active-effect remainders, settings. **All derived stats are
  recomputed on load** by the selectors — never serialized. Additive optional fields (`combat`,
  `estateWealth.subEngines`, dialogue chosen-flags) are added **at their milestone** (§6.8.2), not in the M0
  skeleton.
- **No offline accrual on load.** Active-only: load restores the exact saved `GameState` and resumes; there is
  no time-skip catch-up.

**Persistence lives in `src/persistence/`** (the backend abstraction, the base64 codec, the migration chain,
the newest-wins selector). It is side-effectful and is **never imported by `core`** — `core` only produces the
plain serializable `GameState`; the app layer hands it to persistence.

---

### 6.8.1 Multi-backend redundant save + newest-wins arbitration

- **Atomic write (Q44).** Each backend gets **one atomic `put`** of the whole serialized save — **never** a
  clear-then-rewrite (which can tear). On **any** write rejection (quota, private-mode, a torn write), surface
  a **calm, persistent "couldn't save — export a backup" banner** — never silent loss, never a scary wall.
- **App-identity magic field (Q46).** Every blob carries `app: 'kami-kakushi'`. On load, a blob with a
  missing / foreign / garbled magic field is **rejected fast to recovery** (offer re-import / fresh start) —
  never a silent half-load of someone else's data.
- **Newest-wins selector (FU2).** Reading all backends, the selector picks the newest by the **monotonic
  save-counter** (incremented every save) — this is the **real** selector and is **clock-free**, so it is
  immune to a skewed system clock. The **save-layer timestamp** is **only the tiebreaker** for equal counters;
  it is a **documented core-lint exemption** (`persistence/` may call `Date.now` — metadata, not game logic;
  the core stays deterministic, §6.1/FU2).
- **Forward-version guard.** A backend whose `schemaVersion` **exceeds** the running build's (a future-version
  save in one backend, e.g. an older CDN-stale tab) is **never loaded**; the selector **falls through** to the
  newest **readable** backend, and if none is readable it **rejects to recovery** with the raw bytes preserved
  for re-import in a newer build — never a downgrade that overwrites the real autosave.

---

### 6.8.2 Additive backwards-compatible schema & the migration safety net

- **Additive-optional-fields-with-defaults is the PRIMARY mechanism (Q45/FU5).** New stored fields are added
  as **optional with a default**, never removed or repurposed (protobuf/thrift-style) — so an older save just
  reads as "field absent → default" and **most schema growth needs NO migration at all**. The M0 skeleton
  ships only `{ hp, satiety, attributePoints, character.level=1 }`; `combat` (CombatEncounterState),
  `estateWealth.subEngines`, and dialogue chosen-flags land **additively at their milestone (M3 / M5)** without
  a migration.
- **The ordered migration chain is the SAFETY NET, not the default.** For the rare unavoidable **structural**
  change, `schemaVersion` is stored and an ordered list of `migrate_vN_to_vN+1(save)` steps (each a pure,
  unit-tested function in `persistence/migrations/`) runs in sequence to bring an old save current. A **raw
  pre-migration backup** of the bytes is kept so a failed migration is recoverable. We do **not** guarantee
  cross-major-rewrite compatibility (a future ground-up schema change may legitimately retire very old saves
  with a clear message).
- **Save-safety (Q19/Q49).** Destructive actions (import, fresh-start, and only genuinely-unrecoverable rare
  actions) require an **explicit confirm**, and an **automatic pre-overwrite snapshot** (an extra write into
  the multi-backend set) is taken **before any overwrite** — so an accidental wipe is recoverable.
- **Validate + degrade gracefully.** A corrupt / unreadable save (bad magic, failed parse, failed migration,
  or a forward-version blob, §6.8.1) shows a **calm, explained recovery** (offer re-import or a fresh start),
  **never** a scary "save is kill" wall.

---

## 6.9 Renderer contract (thin DOM, multi-screen, responsive, active-only)

**The renderer is a thin DOM layer in `src/ui/` with zero game logic** (D-013). It is a (near-)pure function
of `GameState`: `render(state, prevState)` reconciles the DOM against the new snapshot; it does **not** compute
outcomes and does **not** mutate state — it only **dispatches intents** back to the core. The combat renderer
animates the deterministic result (filling bars, floating numbers); it never decides the fight.

- **Data-driven surfaces.** Every panel/screen/tab/row/button is described by `core/content/surfaces.ts` with
  an unlock predicate; the renderer shows only `unlockedSurfaces(state)`. "The UI is incremental" is a tunable
  content table, **not** hardcoded view logic. **Reveals are DESIGN-staggered one-at-a-time** — the authored
  unlock schedule is built so surfaces surface inherently one-per-beat (no UI dumps); there is **no runtime
  reveal-queue** in state (FU4, §6.4). Each first-time reveal pushes a diegetic line to the event log (the
  reveal reads as plot); genuine multi-element single-feature reveals are bespoke one-offs designed per case.
- **Multi-screen UI, progressively revealed (canon §H).** There is a real multi-screen shell with navigation
  (e.g. Estate / Village / Wilds / Skills / Combat / Crafting / Quests / Influence / Map / Journal / Settings
  screens), but **nav and screens are revealed as earned** — so it *appears single-screen early* (minute one is
  one verb + the log) and grows into a full multi-screen app. The nav is itself driven by unlock predicates.
  **Distinct non-critical activities get their own top-level nav tab, not a nested panel (Q10):** **Crafting**
  and **Quests** are **top-level tabs** — the main screen stays the active labour/deeds/combat loop. (Still
  revealed one-at-a-time.)
- **Responsive desktop + mobile, NOT hover-dependent (canon §H).** A fluid layout (CSS grid/flex,
  container/media queries) that reflows columns→stacked on narrow screens; **all information reachable without
  hover** — any hover tooltip has a tap/focus-equivalent, and "Shift for more detail" is an *enhancement*, never
  the only path to a value. Touch targets are comfortably sized. **Mobile information architecture (Q19):** the
  **per-tier primary tabs** sit on a bottom bar (the current tier's core surfaces); older / earlier tabs move
  into an **overflow drawer in reveal order** — so the small screen always foregrounds the live loop.
- **Art register = text + inline-SVG motifs + emoji + CSS + a small audio set (D-013 / canon §H; corrects "no
  asset pipeline").** Woodblock-style palette, kanji season tags, colour-coded rarities, CSS flourishes;
  **load-bearing period motifs (pillar / season / rank / rarity marks) are inline SVG** so they render
  identically across OSes, with **emoji kept cosmetic-only**; a small curated audio set (synthesized Web Audio
  + original/CC0; §6.1.1). A small canvas is permitted ONLY for optional ambient FX (seasonal particles),
  **never for logic**. Colour is never the *sole* carrier of meaning (see §6.11).
- **Number formatting = abbreviated K/M/B (canon §H).** Large values display **human-scaled, abbreviated**
  (e.g. `12.4K`, `3.1M`, `2.7B`) — **not** scientific notation (`1.2e7`) and **not** myriad units
  (man/oku). A single shared display formatter in the renderer (a pure helper fed by the selectors) keeps the
  scale legible as koku/coin/pillar numbers climb.
- **Pillar bars, number-flash & seal beats (cross-ref `ui-design.md`).** Pillar bars render
  **distance-to-next-gate** (how far to the next good/great/excellent threshold, not just a raw value); gain /
  loss **number-flash** uses the §2 gain/loss tokens; **vermilion is reserved for rank-up / seal beats** (not
  routine gains). Functional / hint text uses **`--ink-soft`** (WCAG AA on every paper surface; §6.11).
- **Active-only loop.** The app-layer tick loop runs only while the tab is active (driven by
  `requestAnimationFrame` / a paced timer); it computes `dtTicks` from elapsed active time and calls the pure
  `tick`. Backgrounding pauses; there is no offline catch-up. A user **pause** is supported. While the tab is
  **open**, **auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour** run unattended for hours — the "leave it running,
  check progress" feel (FU23) — but this is **strictly active-only**: backgrounding pauses, there is **no**
  offline catch-up, and true auto-producers stay **T3+**.
- **One render path.** Updates go through `render(state)` reconciliation — not scattered manual
  `update_displayed_*` push-calls — which kills the stale-UI class of bug by construction.

---

## 6.10 The DEV play API (`window.__qa`) — playtest by code

**A DEV-only play API on `window.__qa`** (canon: *playtest via code, not synthetic input*; the
`capture-game-states` skill consumes it). It is attached **only when `import.meta.env.DEV`** and is
dead-code-eliminated from the production bundle.

```ts
window.__qa = {
  state(): GameState,                       // read the current snapshot
  dispatch(intent: Intent): void,           // drive any player verb (same intents the UI uses)
  // convenience verb wrappers: rake(), activity(id), travel(id), buy(id), equip(id,slot), ...
  tick(dtTicks: number): void,              // advance the abstract clock by N ticks
  frames(n: number, dtPerFrame?: number): void,  // step N render frames (for capture/pacing)
  pause(): void, resume(): void,            // control the active loop
  newGame(seed?: number): void,             // deterministic fresh start from a seed
  save(): string, load(b64: string): void,  // round-trip the save (base64)
  forceState(patch: DeepPartial<GameState>): void,  // jump to a late unlock / rare outcome / terminal screen
  setSeed(seed: number): void,              // pin RNG for reproducible rare rolls
  pacing(): PacingTelemetry,                // accumulated fun-proxy metrics this run (qa-playtesting.md §3)
  reveals(): RevealLogEntry[],              // what unlocked + when (tick/season) — the reveal-cadence proxy (qa-playtesting.md §1)
  advanceSeason(): void, toRung(id: RankId): void, toTier(n: TierId): void,  // time-compression helpers — fast-forward to a checkpoint (qa-playtesting.md §1)
  selectors: { unlocked(): SurfaceId[]; tier(): TierId; production(): ... },  // read-only derived reads
};
```

This lets us **headlessly drive and observe** the game: assert that each unlock fires at the intended
`GameState`, that pacing milestones (reveal cadence; time-to-next-goal) hit on schedule, that a forced rare
outcome renders correctly, and capture screenshots/recordings of any state for QA — all without synthetic
mouse/keyboard input. The same typed `Intent`s the UI dispatches are what `__qa` drives, so a headless run
exercises the real code paths.

---

## 6.11 Accessibility basics

Solid basics (canon §H), wired so they cannot be an afterthought:

- **Scalable text** (a `textScale` setting on `GameState.settings`, applied via CSS custom property; respects
  the browser/root font size).
- **Colourblind-safe cues:** colour is *never* the sole signal — rarities/severities also carry an icon/emoji
  and/or text label; a `colourblindMode` setting swaps to a safe palette.
- **Contrast (Q48):** functional / hint text uses **`--ink-soft`** (passes **WCAG AA** on every paper
  surface); **`--ink-faint` is decorative-only** (never the sole carrier of a value); the **meter fill is
  darkened** for contrast against the paper.
- **Keyboard + touch:** full keyboard operability (focus order, visible focus ring, no hover-only controls —
  ties to §6.9's not-hover-dependent rule) **and** comfortable touch targets.
- **Reduced motion:** a `reducedMotion` setting (and `prefers-reduced-motion` honoured) downgrades the ambient
  canvas FX and number-float animations.
- **Pause:** the active loop can be paused (also an accessibility/comfort feature).
- **Semantic structure + live region:** the event/story log is a **`polite` ARIA live region scoped to
  narration + milestones** (with an accessible name) — not every number tick — so reveals/important events are
  announced to assistive tech without flooding it; the shell uses **named ARIA landmarks** (`banner` header,
  `navigation` rail, `main` workspace) and labelled controls, plus a **skip-to-log / skip-to-content link** so
  keyboard and screen-reader users jump straight to the content instead of tabbing through the growing
  (~9-entry) nav on every screen.
- **Persistent a11y entry + acceptance pass (Q18):** a **quiet, persistent accessibility entry point** is
  available **from minute one** (not buried late); the QA checklist adds a **large-`textScale` reflow case**
  (the layout must not clip/overlap at max scale) and a **screen-reader acceptance pass**.
- **Language of parts (WCAG 3.1.2):** every Japanese-script run (kanji pillar/rank/season labels — Combat
  **Rank** 武威, etc.) is marked `lang="ja"` and romanized terms (*gōshi*, *rōnin*, *kan'i*) `lang="ja-Latn"`,
  so assistive tech pronounces them instead of reading Unicode glyph names or skipping them; the §6.6
  romanization lint also flags any untagged CJK in rendered strings.
- **Mute:** light ambient beds + UI/event SFX with a mute toggle (canon §H audio note).

---

## 6.12 How this satisfies the project conventions

| Convention (CLAUDE.md) | How §6 satisfies it |
|---|---|
| **Pure-core boundary** | All logic/state/math in `src/core/` with **zero DOM/canvas/window** — enforced by an ESLint boundary rule (build failure, not review). Renderer consumes plain data; one-directional dependency. (§6.2) |
| **Determinism: one RNG** | A single seeded RNG **in `GameState`** as **per-named-stream cursors** (`{ seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} }`), saved/loaded; pure `next`; **stateless day-keyed weather** (not stored); **`Math.random()` AND `Math.pow`/transcendentals lint-banned** in core (integer-pow). Replays are byte-identical across engines (Vitest-asserted). (§6.7, §6.7.1, §6.3) |
| **Single source of truth — generate, don't duplicate** | Content is typed data registries (`core/content`); a content verifier cross-checks ids and canon invariants — now incl. the **V2 hardening** (gate-monotonicity/ceiling, accrual tie-out, per-rung-meter reachability, the **flipped** per-skill-perk magnitude check, the trade-≤⅓-post-combo proof, the hybrid gate-distribution check, the real-name denylist lint); balance/content docs are **generated** into `docs/balance/` + `docs/content/` from the same data and `gen:docs --check` fails the build on drift. (§6.5, §6.6, §6.6.1) |
| **Playtest via code, not synthetic input** | DEV-only `window.__qa` (read state, drive intents, tick/frames, pause, force-state, seed) drives the **real** typed intents headlessly; powers `capture-game-states` and pacing/unlock regression. (§6.10) |
| **Active-only, no offline (D-013/canon §H)** | `tick` takes **abstract whole-integer** ticks (app loop accumulates the fractional remainder); the active-only loop lives in the app layer and drives **auto-resolve combat + auto-repeat labour** while the tab is open (the "leave it running" feel); load resumes the exact saved state with **no** offline accrual code path. (§6.3, §6.8, §6.9) |
| **Lean / high-impact** | One `npm run verify` gate; minimal stored save; no speculative subsystems — the module list maps 1:1 to systems already locked in §§1–5; anything bigger is parked for §7's roadmap. (§6.1, §6.4) |

---

## 6.13 §6 decisions → ADR (proposed)

§6 both **refines D-013** (module layout, the `reduce`/`tick`/rewards contracts, the stored-vs-computed split,
the registry set, the verifier + generated-docs gate, the `__qa` surface) **and** — under the governing
**D-022 (newest-block-wins)** — **implements a set of real V2 locks/reversals** that go beyond buildable
detail:

- **multi-backend redundant save** (Q37) + **atomic write** (Q44) + **app-identity magic field** (Q46) +
  **monotonic-counter newest-wins / timestamp tiebreaker** (FU2) — built full in M0 (FU1);
- **additive backwards-compatible schema** as the primary mechanism, migrations as the safety net (Q45/FU5);
- **per-named-stream RNG cursors** + **stateless day-keyed weather** (Q2/Q3/FU3);
- the **`Math.pow`/transcendental ban** → integer-pow (Q36);
- the **bounded labour→combat verifier flip** (per-skill perk small-magnitude, not ==0; Q6/FU8);
- the **feature-rich data model**: intra-line dialogue `choices[]` + `subEngines` + `character.level` + the
  rung-meter (Q34, Q1, FU14);
- the **no-runtime-reveal-queue** design rule (FU4);
- the **bundled-asset-set correction** (self-hosted OFL fonts, inline-SVG motifs, curated audio, build stamp;
  Q50/Q52/Q38/Q54).

Proposed: record §6 as **D-013a (tech architecture detail)** **plus** the implementation home for **D-023–
D-042** as they touch the data model / save / determinism / verifier. §6 is no longer "elaboration only". Final
ADR numbering is set at integration.

### Flagged for the human — ALL RESOLVED/CONFIRMED (2026-06-25)

> All four items below are **resolved by the human (2026-06-25)**: IndexedDB ✓ · RNG = **splitmix64 + named
> sub-streams** ✓ · **ordered forward migrations + raw backup (no cross-major-rewrite guarantee)** ✓ · `tier`
> **stored** ✓.
>
> **V2 update (2026-06-26, D-022 newest-wins — annotate, don't delete):** items 1–3 are **superseded/refined**
> by the V2 tech decisions; item 4 **stands**. See the per-item notes.

1. **RESOLVED: IndexedDB vs. localStorage → IndexedDB.** Canon/D-013 say IndexedDB; this section commits to it
   (single autosave + base64 export) for robustness. We accept IndexedDB's small async/boilerplate cost over
   localStorage's simpler-but-fragile sync API for a single-slot save. **→ SUPERSEDED (V2, Q37):** IndexedDB is
   now the **primary** of a **multi-backend redundant** layer (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage), not
   the sole store (§6.8 / §6.8.1).
2. **RESOLVED: RNG algorithm → splitmix64 + named sub-streams.** Low-stakes and reversible (it only affects the
   seed→sequence mapping, not any contract), but it pins save reproducibility once content ships — locked as
   **splitmix64** with named sub-streams for the seed format. **→ REFINED (V2, Q2/FU3):** splitmix64 stays, but
   the seed format is now **persisted per-named-stream cursors** `{ seed, cursors:{combat,loot,seasonal,worldgen} }`
   (not "child streams by splitting"), with **stateless day-keyed** weather (§6.7 / §6.7.1).
3. **RESOLVED: `schemaVersion` / migration policy → ordered forward migrations + raw backup.** Support ordered
   forward migrations indefinitely + a pre-migration raw backup; we will *not* guarantee cross-major-rewrite
   save compatibility (so a future ground-up schema change may legitimately retire very old saves with a clear
   message). **→ SUPERSEDED (V2, Q45/FU5):** the **additive backwards-compatible schema** (optional fields with
   defaults) is now the **primary** mechanism, making migrations **rare**; the ordered forward migration chain
   + raw backup remains the **safety net** (§6.8.2).
4. **RESOLVED: `core/state.tier` stored vs. fully derived → stored.** Tier is checkable from the influence
   thresholds, but a tier-up is also a story beat (it fires rewards/log). Store `tier` as the committed value
   (set by the tier-up intent) and treat threshold-progress as derived — rather than re-deriving tier purely
   from pillars on every load. **→ STANDS (V2):** unchanged.

### M7 — Deploy (+ self-hosted fonts · license · About/Credits · content rating · cross-origin save survival)

**Goal:** ship the static build to itch.io as free / pay-what-you-want, with the acknowledged asset set
bundled, the license + credits in place, and the multi-backend save proven inside itch's embed.

**Lands:** `build:itch` (= `vite build` → `dist/` → zipped, §6.1) with the **base path** set for itch's
served subpath and a **single static HTML bundle** (no backend, §6.1/§7.3); a final **export/import save**
round-trip smoke test **plus the multi-backend save-survival smoke on Chromium + WebKit, including the itch
cross-origin-iframe survival test** (deferred here from M0 — the layer itself was built at M0, FU1 / Q37);
**self-hosted OFL fonts** verified (woff2 bundled, **no Google dynamic subsetting / no network**, the OFL
license text bundled — Q52); the **LICENSE file** (permissive code MIT/Apache-2.0 + reserved game content
all-rights-reserved or CC-BY-NC — Q51); the in-product **About/Credits surface** (authorship, the
**commit-SHA build stamp** + build date, font/audio attributions, clean-room attestation — Q54 / §6.1.1);
the **itch content descriptors** (mild thematic: child-disappearance, drowning, debt — Q53); the itch.io page
(free / PWYW per canon §H), the build uploaded, a **fresh-browser smoke test** of the deployed build (load
< 5 s to first interactable; save persists across reload; export/import works).

**Definition of done:** the zipped `dist/` runs from itch.io's static host with the correct base path;
loading the live URL reaches the cold open and the first verb works; the **multi-backend redundant save**
(IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage) persists across reload **and survives inside itch's cross-origin
iframe** (Chromium + WebKit); base64 export/import work against the deployed origin; **fonts are self-hosted
(no Google / no network)**; the **LICENSE file**, the **About/Credits surface + commit-SHA stamp**, and the
**itch content descriptors** are present; **no backend, no network calls**; the release artifact was cut from
a **verify-green** commit.

**Phases / high-level tasks:**

1. **Pin the relative base path + strip the DEV surface** — Done when `vite.config.ts` sets `base: './'` and a production `vite build` emits a single static HTML bundle with hashed JS/CSS (and the **self-hosted woff2 fonts + inline-SVG + the synth/CC0 audio** bundled offline) that resolves from a served subpath, containing NO `__qa`/dev helpers (DEV-only code dead-code-eliminated via `import.meta.env.DEV`). *(§6.1, §6.1.1, §6.10, §7.3, Q52, Q38, Q50)*
2. **Add the `build:itch` script (build → zip) + the build-provenance stamp + the About/Credits + LICENSE** — Done when `npm run build:itch` runs `vite build` then zips the *contents* of `dist/` into an upload-ready artifact, the built bundle stamps the source **commit SHA / version + build date** into the **About/Credits** surface (authorship, font/audio attributions, clean-room attestation — Q54) so any shipped zip is traceable to the commit it was cut from, and a **LICENSE file** (permissive code + reserved content — Q51) is committed before ship. *(§6.1, §6.1.1, §7.3, Q54, Q51)*
3. **Wire the `npm run verify` release gate as the cut-rule** — Done when a local release flow refuses to cut a zip unless `verify` is green (tsc --noEmit + eslint + prettier --check + vitest run + verify-content + gen:docs --check, PLUS the M6 §4.8 headless pacing regression + the fun-proxy gate) and the working tree is clean — i.e. a release artifact can only ever be cut from a verify-green commit; no hosted CI, run locally as the pre-push/release gate. *(§6.1, §7.3, §4.8, §6.6)*
4. **Smoke-test the built artifact offline (itch-subpath + no-network + multi-backend survival)** — Done when the unzipped `dist/` served from a subpath (mimicking itch's served path via `vite preview` / a static server) passes: load < 5 s to first interactable, the first verb (rake rice) works, the **multi-backend save persists across reload (and survives the itch cross-origin iframe — Chromium + WebKit)**, base64 export → clear store → import yields identical state, **fonts load self-hosted with zero network calls**, AND there are zero network calls and no `__qa` surface present. *(§7.3, §6.8, §6.9, §3.1, Q52, FU1, Q37)*

6. **Fresh-browser smoke of the live draft, then publish (human-gated)** — Done when the live draft URL passes the fresh-browser smoke (load < 5 s to first interactable, first verb works, the multi-backend save persists across reload **and survives the cross-origin iframe**, export → clear store → import round-trips identical, no backend / no network calls) and the game is then published free/PWYW — the milestone's go-live beat. *(§7.3, §6.8, FU1, Q37)*
