## 7.3 Deployment

**No backend. Fully static.** Per §6.1 / canon §H: `vite build` emits a static `dist/` (a **single HTML
bundle** + hashed JS/CSS assets + the one bundled asset set, §7.3.1), zipped and uploaded to **itch.io**.

- **Static itch.io build.** `npm run build:itch` = `vite build` + zip **the *contents* of** `dist/` (so `index.html` sits at the **archive root**, never nested under a `dist/` folder — itch.io requires `index.html` at the zip root or the embed shows a blank frame). itch.io serves the unzipped
  bundle from a project subpath, so Vite's **`base`** must be set to a **relative base** (`base: './'`) so
  asset URLs resolve under itch's served path — this is the single most common static-host break and is
  pinned in `vite.config.ts`. The DEV play API and any dev-only helpers are stripped via
  `import.meta.env.DEV` (dead-code-eliminated), so the shipped bundle carries **no** `__qa` surface.
- **The save path (multi-backend + export/import).** Persistence is a **MULTI-BACKEND redundant save**
  (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage behind one abstraction, **atomic single-`put` writes** to every
  available backend) with an **app-identity magic field** (`app:'kami-kakushi'` → reject-to-recovery on a
  bad / foreign / forward-version save), a **monotonic save-counter** newest-wins selector (the save-layer
  timestamp is the tiebreaker only — a documented core-lint exemption, FU2), and a **backwards-compatible
  additive schema** (migrations rare — never remove/repurpose a field). On top of that sits **base64
  export/import** to a text file (§6.8). Because itch.io is a static host with no server, the base64 export
  is the **portable** backup / hand-off path — the player's safety net against a cleared browser store; the
  redundant backends are the everyday safety net. Import validates + migrates (ordered migrations,
  pre-migration raw backup; a corrupt save degrades gracefully, never a "save is kill" wall).
- **The `npm run verify` release gate.** The **same** one-command gate that guards every commit (§6.1) is
  the release gate: `tsc --noEmit && eslint . && prettier --check . && vitest run && verify-content &&
  gen:docs --check` — i.e. **typecheck + unit tests + the content-verifier (incl. the K/M/B + macron + the
  canon-invariant machine checks) + lints**, plus the **§4.8 headless pacing regression + the fun-proxy
  gate** added at M6. A release artifact is **only ever cut from a verify-green commit**; `verify` is run
  **locally** as the pre-push / release gate (**no hosted CI, no deploy automation** — confirmed by the
  human 2026-06-25).
- **How to ship to itch.io (brief).** (1) `npm run verify` green; (2) `npm run build:itch` → a zipped
  `dist/`; (3) **deploy-checklist (V2):** confirm the **LICENSE file** exists (§7.3.2); verify the
  **fonts are self-hosted** (woff2 bundled, **no Google / no network**, §7.3.1); confirm the
  **About/Credits surface + commit-SHA stamp** is present (§7.3.2); set the **itch content descriptors**
  (child-disappearance, drowning, debt, §7.3.2); run the **multi-backend save-survival smoke on Chromium +
  WebKit** (incl. the cross-origin-iframe survival); (4) on itch.io, create / edit the project, set **Kind =
  HTML**, upload the zip, tick **"This file will be played in the browser,"** set the viewport (a sensible
  default frame; the layout is responsive per §6.9), and set pricing to **free / pay-what-you-want** (canon
  §H); (5) save as a draft, open the draft URL, run the **fresh-browser smoke test** (load < 5 s to first
  interactable; rake rice; reload → autosave persists; export → clear store → import → identical); (6)
  publish.

> **Out of scope for v1 deployment:** any server, account system, cloud save, analytics backend, or
> network call. The game is wholly client-side and offline-capable after first load (active-only; no
> offline *progress*, but it runs with no network).

### 7.3.1 Assets (the acknowledged small set — corrects "no asset pipeline")

The earlier "text + emoji + CSS, **no asset pipeline**" framing is **corrected** (§6.1.1): v1 ships **one
small, curated, fully-bundled asset set** (everything self-hosted in `dist/`, works offline and under itch's
relative-base iframe — no runtime fetch, no network):

- **Self-hosted OFL fonts (Q52).** The display/body typefaces are SIL **Open Font License** fonts,
  **self-hosted** (woff2 committed into the repo / bundled by Vite) — **no Google dynamic subsetting**
  (which breaks offline play and itch's relative base URL). The **OFL license text is bundled** alongside
  them, and the Reserved-Font-Name rule is cleared.
- **Inline SVG for load-bearing period motifs (Q38).** Pillar / season / rank / rarity marks that must be
  **identical across OSes** are **inline SVG** (not emoji); **emoji stay cosmetic-only**.
- **A small curated audio set (Q50).** A light ambient bed + UI/event SFX, made of **synthesized Web Audio**
  (procedural, code-only) plus a few **original / CC0** samples — the ONE acknowledged sample set. Bundled
  into `dist/`; mute toggle per §6.11.

### 7.3.2 About/Credits, licensing & content rating

- **About/Credits surface (Q54, §6.1.1).** A small in-product surface (on the Settings/About screen, §2.21)
  carries: **authorship**, the **commit-SHA build stamp** + build date (injected by the build for
  reproducible bug reports), the **font / audio attributions** (OFL + CC0/original), and a **clean-room
  attestation** — pre-satisfying the attribution duties for the SVG / audio / font decisions.
- **License split (Q51).** **Permissive code** (MIT or Apache-2.0) **+ reserved game content**
  (all-rights-reserved, or CC-BY-NC) — the code is reusable, the world/writing/art is not. A **LICENSE file
  is committed before M7** (the deploy-checklist verifies it exists).
- **itch content descriptors (Q53).** The itch.io page declares the **mild thematic descriptors**:
  **child-disappearance, drowning, debt** — set as a §7.3 / M7 deploy-checklist step (human-gated upload).

---

## 7.4 Risk register + scope-risk posture

### 7.4.1 Top risks

| # | Risk | Likelihood / impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| **R1 — Scope creep on T2** (the widest, warmest tier: region map + Origin faction + two payoffs + Kuzuhara + rivals) blows the timeline | **High / High** | Hold the **~6–8-node** cut-set and the **hard caps** (exactly 2 neighbouring valleys; 2–3-man detail; ~5 mobs) as *invariants*, not suggestions; build T2 **rung-by-rung** (M5) so progress is always verify-green; park anything not on the §1.7.1 spine list. **v1 ships full T0–T2 — no pre-planned descope (§7.4.2)**; if genuinely blocked, the forward-migratable save (§6.8) lets a later update add tiers. |
| **R2 — Balance-tuning time** (meeting the LOCKED 4.5/8/16-h **floors** + the ≥30-min floor + ≈70/30 split across 24 rungs) is open-ended | **Medium / Medium** | **The budget is a FLOOR, not a target (FU18):** the grind is a **minimum**, the game can/should be **longer** — so the §4.8 curve is derived to be **AT LEAST** the floor and M6 tunes *yields* against a one-sided bar, **lengthening / interleaving** content rather than hitting a fixed total. The **headless pacing regression** (§6.10) fails on **undershoot only** (overshoot never fails), so tuning is measured, not vibes; every number lives in `balance.ts` (§6.4) and reflows with **no save migration** — tuning is cheap and continuous, not a big-bang at the end. |
| **R3 — Save migration / loss** (a stored-shape change or a cleared store orphans players' saves) | **Low / High** | Store **only non-derivable state** (§6.4) so the migratable surface is minimal; the **MULTI-BACKEND redundant write** (IndexedDB + localStorage + sessionStorage, atomic single-`put`) + the **monotonic-counter + timestamp newest-wins** selector + a **backwards-compatible additive schema** (never remove/repurpose a field) + the **magic-field reject-to-recovery** together make save-loss very unlikely; **ordered, unit-tested migrations** + a **pre-migration raw backup**; base64 export as the player's own safety net; degrade gracefully on a bad save (never a hard wall). The **itch cross-origin-iframe survival** is tested at M7 on Chromium + WebKit. The stored/computed split (§6.4) means balance retunes **never** migrate. |
| **R4 — Art / feel** (text + emoji + CSS + the small asset set must read as a *coherent woodblock world*, not a spreadsheet) | **Medium / Medium** | The art register is locked **low-risk** — **a small curated asset set** (inline-SVG load-bearing motifs + a synth/CC0 audio bed + self-hosted OFL fonts, §7.3.1), not a full asset pipeline; a dedicated **M6 polish pass** + a **`capture-game-states` audit** sweep catch feel regressions; the diegetic event log carries most of the "feel," so feel scales with *writing* (a known quantity) more than with art production. |
| **R5 — The combat slice is the densest stretch** and could stall the whole roadmap | **Medium / Medium** | **M2a / M2b are fixed milestones** split up front at the R3→R4 seam (M2a = auto-resolve + first fight; M2b = bestiary/gear/durability), so the combat slice is two shippable, verify-green checkpoints by design; the deterministic seeded auto-battler is **unit-testable in isolation** (§6.7) before it's wired to the UI; the first-fight win-rate (20–35% at adequate satiety ≥~0.7) and soft-setback shape are **LOCKED**, so the target is fixed. |
| **R6 — Fun** (the LOCKED minimum-grind ships *balanced* but reads as a **slog**) | **Medium / Medium** | The **fun-proxies** (dead-time, reward/unlock cadence, visible-next-goal, first-5-min hook + the win-rate bands) are instrumented **report-only at M1** and become a **verify GATE at M6** (FU9 / Q4 / FU19), so a slog is caught by machine, not vibes; the **FU18 interleave-don't-brick-wall** posture + the tab-open **auto-resolve / auto-repeat** "leave it running, check progress" loop (FU23) keep the long grind palatable; the human is the final fun & taste arbiter (capture-game-states audit). |
| **R7 — Perf / memory** (a sustained multi-hour active session degrades per-tick or render time) | **Low / Medium** | A **perf/memory acceptance criterion** (a sustained-runtime **per-tick + render-ms budget**) is **deferred to after M0/M1 profiling** (Q56) — tracked here so it isn't forgotten; the active-only loop (whole-integer ticks, no offline catch-up) and the thin active-only renderer (§6.2/§6.9) keep the surface small. Real numbers + the gate land once M0/M1 profiling exists. |
| **R8 — Bounded perks / combat-reveal spread** (the no-global-cap per-skill perks let labour over-contribute to combat; or the combat reveal-ladder UI-dumps) | **Medium / Medium** | **Balance risk accepted (FU8):** the per-skill perks have **no hard global cap** but are bounded by **small per-perk magnitudes × incremental skill-unlock pacing × holistic enemy-scaling** (gear/level/attrs all grow together), and the **FLIPPED no-hidden-edge verifier** asserts per-perk smallness **AND** conditioning == 0 (§6.6); the combat reveal ladder is **design-staggered one-per-beat** across M2a→M5 (FU12, §7.2.0) so no milestone UI-dumps — proven by the authored unlock schedule + the verifier. |

### 7.4.2 Scope-risk posture — no pre-planned descope

**v1 = full T0–T2, non-negotiable.** The human chose **not** to pre-plan a reduced-scope cut (no "minimum
shippable T0–T1" fallback, no cut-down ladder) — we build to the full T0–T2 target and ship it complete
(plus the §3.7.1 T3 stub cliff-hanger). We do **not** design a T0–T1 fallback.

> **Holding scope:** every milestone stays **verify-green, deterministic, and a complete playable arc to its
> frontier**, and we hold the §7.1 cut-set + hard caps as *invariants* (this is how we protect the budget —
> by trimming *breadth within a tier*, never by dropping a tier). If a milestone is **genuinely blocked**,
> the forward-migratable save (§6.8) plus **no reset** means a later update can add tiers without orphaning
> anyone — but that is a recovery path, not a plan: **v1 ships complete T0–T2.**

---

## 7.5 §7 decisions → ADR (proposed)

§7 still opens **no new design decisions** — it sequences the already-locked design (§§1–6) into an
execution plan, now **reshaped for V2** under the governing **D-022** (the Block L/M decisions supersede
conflicting prior locks; annotate, don't delete). Proposed ADR: record **the v1 scope (§7.1) + the M0…M7
build order (§7.2, with the combat slice as the fixed milestones M2a/M2b) + the static-itch.io deployment +
verify-gate (§7.3) + the no-pre-planned-descope scope posture (§7.4.2: full T0–T2 is non-negotiable)** as
**D-017 — v1 execution plan** (D-016 is the §4 balance locks — distinct, now **annotated by FU18: the budget
is a FLOOR, not a ceiling**), an execution checkpoint rather than a design reversal. The V2 reshape carried
into D-017 is: the **FULL multi-backend save in M0** (FU1), the **numeric rung-meter + sequential
phase-1/phase-2** from M1+ (FU6/FU7), the **combat reveal-ladder spread M2a→M5** with combat content
sub-slices in M4/M5 (FU12/FU13), the **hybrid tier-gate + phase-2 grind** (Q7/FU10), **E3 + broadened combos
in M5** (Q8/FU20), and **fun-proxies (report-only M1 → gate M6) + a11y + the small asset set + license +
credits + content-rating at M6/M7** (FU9/Q4/Q38/Q50/Q52/Q51/Q53/Q54). "Combat Standing" is renamed **"Combat
Rank"** project-wide (Q9). Final ADR numbering is set at integration. **Approving §7 is the gate that unlocks
code** (per the PRD preamble: *no code until §7 is approved*).

### Flagged for the human — ALL RESOLVED (2026-06-25)

> All items below are **resolved by the human (2026-06-25)**; none of it changes *what the game is* (§§1–6),
> only *how/when we build and ship it*. (The Block L/M V2 decisions, 2026-06-26, further re-cut M0–M7 detail
> per D-022 — see §7.5 above — but the five posture calls below still hold.)

1. **RESOLVED: Milestone granularity / the M2 split → fixed M2a / M2b milestones.** The combat slice is **two
   fixed, separate milestones up front** (M2a = auto-resolve + first fight; M2b = bestiary / equipment /
   loot→craft) at the R3→R4 seam — **not** a conditional "agent's call at build time" split. (The later
   combat **content** sub-slices in M4/M5 — 2nd & 3rd weapon lines — are **additive**, not a re-split of M2.)
2. **RESOLVED: The cut-down floor → no pre-planned cut.** **Full T0–T2 is non-negotiable**; we do **not**
   pre-plan a reduced-scope cut and we do **not** design a T0–T1 minimum-shippable fallback (§7.4.2). If
   genuinely blocked, the forward-migratable save (§6.8) lets a later update add tiers — but v1 ships
   complete T0–T2.
3. **RESOLVED: itch.io page specifics → confirmed.** **Free / pay-what-you-want** (canon §H), **Kind = HTML /
   play-in-browser**, **relative `base: './'`**, and a sensible responsive default frame. Confirmed.
4. **RESOLVED: CI host for `npm run verify` → local verify gate, no hosted CI.** The release gate is
   `npm run verify` + the §4.8 pacing regression (+ the M6 fun-proxy gate), run **locally** as the
   pre-push/release gate — **no hosted CI, no deploy automation** — sufficient for a solo/agentic workflow.
5. **RESOLVED: "How to ship" → manual upload, confirmed.** The itch.io upload is a **manual** step (§7.3) —
   outward-facing / hard-to-reverse, so per CLAUDE.md it needs human approval each release. **No** deploy
   automation (no itch CLI / butler pipeline) for v1. Manual upload is the intended release process.
